  5→import androidx.lifecycle.MutableLiveData

  8→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostImmutableModel
  9→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus
 10→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.DRAFT
 11→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.PUBLISHED
 12→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.post.PostStatus.SCHEDULED
 13→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore
 14→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period
 15→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.OFF
 16→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.ONE_HOUR
 17→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.store.PostSchedulingNotificationStore.SchedulingReminderModel.Period.TEN_MINUTES

 23→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.Event
 24→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
 25→import java.util.Calendar
 26→
 27→abstract class PublishSettingsViewModel
 28→constructor(
 29→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
 30→    private val postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
 31→    private val localeManagerWrapper: LocaleManagerWrapper,
 32→    private val postSchedulingNotificationStore: PostSchedulingNotificationStore,
 33→    private val siteStore: SiteStore
 34→) : ViewModel() {
 35→    protected var editPostRepository: EditPostRepository? = null
 36→        private set
 37→
 38→    var canPublishImmediately: Boolean = false
 39→
 40→    var year: Int? = null
 41→        private set
 42→    var month: Int? = null
 43→        private set
 44→    var day: Int? = null
 45→        private set
 46→    var hour: Int? = null
 47→        private set
 48→    var minute: Int? = null
 49→        private set
 50→
 51→    private val _onDatePicked = MutableLiveData<Event<Unit>>()
 52→    val onDatePicked: LiveData<Event<Unit>> = _onDatePicked
 53→    private val _onPublishedDateChanged = MutableLiveData<Event<Calendar>>()
 54→    val onPublishedDateChanged: LiveData<Event<Calendar>> = _onPublishedDateChanged
 55→    private val _onPostStatusChanged = MutableLiveData<PostStatus>()
 56→    val onPostStatusChanged: LiveData<PostStatus> = _onPostStatusChanged
 57→    private val _onUiModel = MutableLiveData<PublishUiModel>()
 58→    val onUiModel: LiveData<PublishUiModel> = _onUiModel
 59→
 60→    @Suppress("VariableNaming")
 61→    protected val _onToast = MutableLiveData<Event<String>>()
 62→    val onToast: LiveData<Event<String>> = _onToast
 63→    private val _onShowNotificationDialog = MutableLiveData<Event<Period?>>()
 64→    val onShowNotificationDialog: LiveData<Event<Period?>> = _onShowNotificationDialog
 65→    private val _onNotificationTime = MutableLiveData<Period?>()
 66→    val onNotificationTime: LiveData<Period?> = _onNotificationTime
 67→    private val _onNotificationAdded = MutableLiveData<Event<Notification>>()
 68→    val onNotificationAdded: LiveData<Event<Notification>> = _onNotificationAdded
 69→    private val _onAddToCalendar = MutableLiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>>()
 70→    val onAddToCalendar: LiveData<Event<CalendarEvent>> = _onAddToCalendar
 71→
 72→    open fun start(postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
 73→        editPostRepository = postRepository
 74→        val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
 75→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 76→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar)
 77→        onPostStatusChanged(postRepository?.getPost())
 78→    }
 79→
 80→    fun onPostStatusChanged(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
 81→        canPublishImmediately = postModel?.let {
 82→            PostUtils.shouldPublishImmediatelyOptionBeAvailable(
 83→                it.status
 84→            )
 85→        } ?: false
 86→        updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    fun publishNow() {
 90→        val currentCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 91→        updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(currentCalendar)
 92→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(currentCalendar))
 93→    }
 94→
 95→    fun onTimeSelected(selectedHour: Int, selectedMinute: Int) {
 96→        this.hour = selectedHour
 97→        this.minute = selectedMinute
 98→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
 99→        calendar.set(year!!, month!!, day!!, hour!!, minute!!)
100→        _onPublishedDateChanged.postValue(Event(calendar))
101→    }
102→
103→    fun onDateSelected(year: Int, month: Int, dayOfMonth: Int) {
104→        this.year = year
105→        this.month = month
106→        this.day = dayOfMonth
107→        _onDatePicked.postValue(Event(Unit))
108→    }
109→
110→    fun onShowDialog(postModel: PostImmutableModel) {
111→        if (areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)) {
112→            val currentPeriod = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
113→            _onShowNotificationDialog.postValue(Event(currentPeriod))
114→        } else {
115→            _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.post_notification_error)))
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    fun updatePost(updatedDate: Calendar, postRepository: EditPostRepository?) {
120→        postRepository?.updateAsync({ postModel ->
121→            val dateCreated = DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromDate(updatedDate.time)
122→            postModel.setDateCreated(dateCreated)
123→            val initialPostStatus = postRepository.status
124→            val isPublishDateInTheFuture = PostUtils.isPublishDateInTheFuture(dateCreated)
125→            var finalPostStatus = initialPostStatus
126→            if (initialPostStatus == DRAFT && isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
127→                // The previous logic was setting the status twice, once from draft to published and when the user
128→                // picked the time, it set it from published to scheduled. This is now done in one step.
129→                finalPostStatus = SCHEDULED
130→            } else if (initialPostStatus == PUBLISHED && postRepository.isLocalDraft) {
131→                // if user was changing dates for a local draft (not saved yet), only way to have it set to PUBLISH
132→                // is by running into the if case above. So, if they're updating the date again by calling
133→                // `updatePublishDate()`, get it back to DRAFT.
134→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
135→            } else if (initialPostStatus == SCHEDULED && !isPublishDateInTheFuture) {
136→                // if this is a SCHEDULED post and the user is trying to Back-date it now, let's update it to DRAFT.
137→                // The other option was to make it published immediately but, let the user actively do that rather than
138→                // having the app be smart about it - we don't want to accidentally publish a post.
139→                finalPostStatus = DRAFT
140→                // show toast only once, when time is shown
141→                _onToast.postValue(Event(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.editor_post_converted_back_to_draft)))
142→            }
143→            postModel.setStatus(finalPostStatus.toString())
144→            _onPostStatusChanged.postValue(finalPostStatus)
145→            val scheduledTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postRepository.id)
146→            updateNotifications(postRepository, scheduledTime)
147→            true
148→        }, onCompleted = { postModel, result ->
149→            if (result == UpdatePostResult.Updated) {
150→                updateUiModel(postModel = postModel)
151→            }
152→        })
153→    }
154→
155→    fun updateUiModel(postModel: PostImmutableModel?) {
156→        if (postModel != null) {
157→            val notificationTime = postSchedulingNotificationStore.getSchedulingReminderPeriod(postModel.id)
158→            val publishDateLabel = postSettingsUtils.getPublishDateLabel(postModel)
159→            val now = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis - 10000
160→            val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
161→                ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
162→            val enableNotification = areNotificationsEnabled(postModel)
163→            val showNotification = dateCreated > now
164→            val notificationLabel = if (enableNotification && showNotification) {
165→                notificationTime.toLabel()
166→            } else {
167→                R.string.post_notification_off
168→            }
169→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(
170→                publishDateLabel,
171→                notificationLabel = notificationLabel,
172→                notificationEnabled = enableNotification,
173→                notificationVisible = showNotification
174→            )
175→        } else {
176→            _onUiModel.value = PublishUiModel(resourceProvider.getString(R.string.immediately))
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    fun onNotificationCreated(scheduleTime: Period?) {
181→        _onNotificationTime.value = scheduleTime
182→    }
183→
184→    fun scheduleNotification(postRepository: EditPostRepository, notificationTime: Period) {
185→        updateNotifications(postRepository, notificationTime)
186→        updateUiModel(postRepository.getPost())
187→    }
188→
189→    fun onAddToCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository) {
190→        val startTime = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated).time
191→        val site = siteStore.getSiteByLocalId(postRepository.localSiteId)
192→        val title = resourceProvider.getString(
193→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_title,
194→            postRepository.title
195→        )
196→        val appName = resourceProvider.getString(R.string.app_name)
197→        val description = resourceProvider.getString(
198→            R.string.calendar_scheduled_post_description,
199→            postRepository.title,
200→            site?.name ?: site?.url ?: "",
201→            appName,
202→            postRepository.link
203→        )
204→        _onAddToCalendar.value = Event(CalendarEvent(title, description, startTime))
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(postRepository: EditPostRepository): Calendar {
208→        val calendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()
209→        val dateCreated = postRepository.dateCreated
210→        // Set the currently selected time if available
211→        if (!TextUtils.isEmpty(dateCreated)) {
212→            calendar.time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(dateCreated)
213→            calendar.timeZone = localeManagerWrapper.getTimeZone()
214→        }
215→        return calendar
216→    }
217→
218→    private fun updateNotifications(
219→        postRepository: EditPostRepository,
220→        schedulingReminderPeriod: Period = OFF
221→    ) {
222→        postSchedulingNotificationStore.deleteSchedulingReminders(postRepository.id)
223→        if (schedulingReminderPeriod != OFF) {
224→            val notificationId = postSchedulingNotificationStore.schedule(postRepository.id, schedulingReminderPeriod)
225→            val scheduledCalendar = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().apply {
226→                timeInMillis = System.currentTimeMillis()
227→                time = DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postRepository.dateCreated)
228→                val scheduledMinutes = when (schedulingReminderPeriod) {
229→                    ONE_HOUR -> -60
230→                    TEN_MINUTES -> -10
231→                    WHEN_PUBLISHED -> 0
232→                    OFF -> return
233→                }
234→                add(Calendar.MINUTE, scheduledMinutes)
235→            }
236→            if (scheduledCalendar.after(localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar())) {
237→                notificationId?.let {
238→                    _onNotificationAdded.postValue(Event(Notification(notificationId, scheduledCalendar.timeInMillis)))
239→                }
240→            }
241→        }
242→    }
243→
244→    private fun areNotificationsEnabled(postModel: PostImmutableModel): Boolean {
245→        val futureTime = localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().timeInMillis + 6000
246→        val dateCreated = (DateTimeUtils.dateFromIso8601(postModel.dateCreated)
247→            ?: localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar().time).time
248→        return dateCreated > futureTime
249→    }
250→
251→    private fun Period.toLabel(): Int {
252→        return when (this) {
253→            OFF -> R.string.post_notification_off
254→            ONE_HOUR -> R.string.post_notification_one_hour_before
255→            TEN_MINUTES -> R.string.post_notification_ten_minutes_before
256→            WHEN_PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_notification_when_published
257→        }
258→    }
259→
260→    private fun updateDateAndTimeFromCalendar(startCalendar: Calendar) {
261→        year = startCalendar.get(Calendar.YEAR)
262→        month = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MONTH)
263→        day = startCalendar.get(Calendar.DAY_OF_MONTH)
264→        hour = startCalendar.get(Calendar.HOUR_OF_DAY)
265→        minute = startCalendar.get(Calendar.MINUTE)
266→    }
267→
268→    data class PublishUiModel(
269→        val publishDateLabel: String,
270→        val notificationLabel: Int = R.string.post_notification_off,
271→        val notificationEnabled: Boolean = false,
272→        val notificationVisible: Boolean = true
273→    )
274→
275→    data class Notification(val id: Int, val scheduledTime: Long)
276→
277→    data class CalendarEvent(val title: String, val description: String, val startTime: Long)
278→}
279→

107→
108→    @MainThread
109→    fun <T> update(action: (PostModel) -> T): T {
110→        reportTransactionState(true)
111→        val result = action(requireNotNull(post))
112→        reportTransactionState(false)
113→        _postChanged.value = Event(requireNotNull(post))

117→    fun updateAsync(
118→        action: (PostModel) -> Boolean,
119→        onCompleted: ((PostImmutableModel, UpdatePostResult) -> Unit)? = null
120→    ) {
121→        launch {
122→            reportTransactionState(true)
123→            val isUpdated = withContext(bgDispatcher) {
124→                action(requireNotNull(post))

126→            reportTransactionState(false)
127→            if (isUpdated) {
128→                requireNotNull(post).let {

152→                Arrays.toString(
153→                    Thread.currentThread().stackTrace
154→                )

156→            AppLog.e(T.EDITOR, message)

198→                post?.changesConfirmedContentHashcode != postSnapshot?.changesConfirmedContentHashcode
199→    }

[thinking: Now I can see the issue. Looking at line 86 in EditPostRepository.kt:

```kotlin
val dateCreated: String
    get() = post!!.dateCreated
```

This will throw a NullPointerException if `post` is null wh...]
Now I understand the issue. Let me trace through the stack trace:

1. `EditPostRepository.getDateCreated()` at line 86 accesses `post!!.dateCreated` which throws NPE when `post` is null
2. This is called from `PublishSettingsViewModel.getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar()` at line 209 which accesses `postRepository.dateCreated`
3. This is called from `PublishSettingsViewModel.start()` at line 74

The problem is that in the `start` method, while `postRepository` is null-checked, the `post` object inside the `EditPostRepository` might still be null. The `EditPostRepository.dateCreated` property uses `post!!.dateCreated` which will throw a NPE if `post` is null.

[thinking: The changes look good. Let me summarize what was done:## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The `NullPointerException` was occurring when `PublishSettingsViewModel.start()` was called with an `EditPos...]
The fix is complete. Here's a summary of the changes made:

## Summary

### Root Cause
The `NullPointerException` occurred in `PublishSettingsViewModel.start()` when called with an `EditPostRepository` that had no post loaded (i.e., the internal `post` field was `null`). The existing code checked if `postRepository` was null, but not whether the repository actually contained a post:

```kotlin
// Before: Only checked if postRepository != null, but not if it has a post
val startCalendar = postRepository?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
```

When `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar()` tried to access `postRepository.dateCreated`, it internally executed `post!!.dateCreated` which threw the NPE.

### Fix Applied
**File:** `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/posts/PublishSettingsViewModel.kt` (line 74)

```kotlin
// After: Also checks if the repository has a post before accessing dateCreated
val startCalendar = postRepository?.takeIf { it.hasPost() }?.let { getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar(it) }
```

This ensures `getCurrentPublishDateAsCalendar()` is only called when the repository both exists AND has a valid post loaded. If either condition fails, the fallback `localeManagerWrapper.getCurrentCalendar()` is used.

### Test Updates
**File:** `WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/posts/EditPostPublishSettingsViewModelTest.kt`

1. Added `whenever(editPostRepository.hasPost()).thenReturn(true)` to the setup method to ensure existing tests continue to work
2. Added a new test case `on start sets current date when repository has no post` that specifically verifies the fixed scenario - when a repository exists but has no post loaded, it should gracefully fall back to the current calendar instead of crashing

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.01 (commit: 361bc30a).

## Issue to Resolve

Sentry Issue: [JETPACK-ANDROID-92S](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?referrer=github_integration)

    at kotlinx.coroutines.sync.MutexImpl$CancellableContinuationWithOwner$tryResume$token$1.invoke(Mutex.kt:252)
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:259)

CompletionHandlerException: Exception in resume onCancellation handler for CancellableContinuation(DispatchedContinuation[Dispatchers.IO, Continuation at org.wordpress.android.ui.uploads.UploadStarter$upload$2.invokeSuspend(UploadStarter.kt:211)@df58903]){Completed}@c9c7080
    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.callOnCancellation(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:264)

    at kotlinx.coroutines.CancellableContinuationImpl.cancelCompletedResult$kotlinx_coroutines_core(CancellableContinuationImpl.kt:180)

This is not a duplicate of #17463 since this is now happening on coroutines 1.7.1 which fixed the underlying root cause of that other ticket. This has a similar stacktrace, but it looks like we might be misusing the Mutex in our `UploadStarter` implementation.

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

**TLDR:** I think our code is silently making the crash look like it’s an internal Mutex problem but in reality, we might be calling `unlock` unnecessarily when the Mutex internals are not expecting it. We should remove [this line](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) and I think it might solve our issues on coroutines 1.7.1.

Based on the [breadcrumbs](https://a8c.sentry.io/issues/4295575735/?project=5731682&referrer=issue-stream&sort=freq&statsPeriod=14d&stream_index=1#breadcrumbs) I believe the actual issue is that we tried to [start the post upload service](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L175-L179) while the app was in the background and the system didn’t let us, which caused all `upload` jobs (created [here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L138-L142)) to be canceled since the [Parent Job](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L68) is not a `SupervisorJob`.

This made all jobs (except the first one which failed because of an `IllegalStateException`) [fall here](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L183-L187) (as can be seen in the breadcrumb logs).

**What I first thought could be happening is:**

We use the `bgDispatcher` ([Dispatchers.Default](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/e03d208247c7487a7497f873d91f6b0e52a3622e/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/modules/ThreadModule.kt#L40-L42)) which has several threads in the pool and we use the same `mutex` for all upload jobs. Because of that, multiple coroutines (running in different threads) are suspended awaiting the to [acquire](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L196-L201C6) the semaphore (which [creates a queue of segments](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L285-L315)).

Since multiple jobs are being canceled at once, I think that the cancellation was not able to be properly propagated and the `segment` state for one of them was not marked as `CANCELLED` making the `Semaphore#tryResumeNextFromQueue` ([which is called on](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/1.7.1/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L265) `Semaphore#release` [via the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L216) `Mutex#unlock`) [call the](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L329-L341) `tryResumeAcquire`. Which in turn [calls](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Semaphore.kt#L347C23-L347C23) `Mutex#tryResume` but since that is canceled it runs the `onCancellation` [callback](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) when trying to resume the underlying continuation, which is [where the crash is happening](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L250-L262).

But I am not 100% sure about that, as I feel like this scenario would be pretty common for a synchronization mechanism using coroutines (like `Mutex` and `Semaphore`), which is why I believe that `onCancellation` callback also unlocks the `Mutex`, in case of that late propagation of the cancellation to the mutex `Segment`.

**So, considering that the code makes sense and works (hopefully) what I think might be the issue is:**

We also have a [call to](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) `unlock` on our side and from the breadcrumbs we know that the code is reaching that line. So it might be that this `unlock` is actually unlocking the mutex **at the same time as** the whole cancellation flow I mentioned above is happening (_which would be possible because of the multiple threads in the Dispatcher pool_), so when the cancelation reaches the `onCancellation` [callback here](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L253-L255) our code already unlocked it, causing the crash to happen in the `Mutex` internals.

This is my best guess, but the Mutex/Semaphore code is a bit complex and I didn’t fully understand it yet (and probably never will 😆).

### Suggestion
Remove that `unlock` [call ASAP](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/blob/1b5e1f3fc97e13d0068ea803d5df7777f421180b/WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt#L186) as we really don’t need it since we use `withLock` which is better in 2 ways:
1. It makes sure to `unlock` when exceptions happen ([try/finally](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/02382479398518ea1c4507170aa92692595b6839/kotlinx-coroutines-core/common/src/sync/Mutex.kt#L120-L131))
2. It only calls `unlock` if it was successfully locked before running the `action`, so if my hypothesis above is correct, the internal `lockSuspend` code will cause a cancellation before reaching that `try/finally` block

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/uploads/UploadStarter.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

conversationHistory.content:
[thinking: Now I understand the issue. Looking at line 186, there's an explicit call to `mutex.unlock()` in the `catch` block for `CancellationException`. 

The problem is that:
1. `mutex.withLock` already handl...]
Now I understand the issue. Let me analyze the problem:

1. The code uses `mutex.withLock` at line 150, which internally uses a try/finally block to ensure the mutex is properly unlocked after the action completes (whether normally or exceptionally).

2. At line 186, there's an explicit `mutex.unlock()` call in the `catch (e: CancellationException)` block.

3. The problem is that when a `CancellationException` occurs:
   - If it happens **inside** the `withLock` block, the `withLock`'s internal finally block will already unlock the mutex
   - If it happens **before** successfully acquiring the lock (during `lockSuspend`), the mutex was never locked, so calling `unlock()` is invalid
   - The coroutines library also has internal cancellation handlers that may call unlock

4. This causes a race condition where multiple unlock calls can occur, leading to the "This mutex is not locked" error.

The fix is to remove the redundant `mutex.unlock()` call since `withLock` already handles all unlock scenarios properly through its try/finally block.

470→    @Test
471→    fun `verify number of auto upload attempts count is incremented when upload invoked`() = test {
472→        // Given
473→        val siteModel = createSiteModel()
474→        val postModel = createLocallyChangedPostModel()
475→        defaultSetup(siteModel, postModel)
476→        val dispatcher: Dispatcher = mock()
477→
478→        // When
479→        createUploadStarter(dispatcher = dispatcher).queueUploadFromSite(siteModel)
480→
481→        // Then
482→        argumentCaptor<Action<PostModel>>().apply {
483→            verify(dispatcher, times(1)).dispatch(capture())
484→            Assertions.assertThat(allValues[0].payload).isEqualTo(postModel)
485→            Assertions.assertThat(allValues[0].type).isEqualTo(UploadAction.INCREMENT_NUMBER_OF_AUTO_UPLOAD_ATTEMPTS)
486→        }
487→    }

489→    private fun defaultSetup(siteModel: SiteModel, postModel: PostModel) = test {
490→        whenever(postStore.getPostsWithLocalChanges(any())).thenReturn(listOf(postModel))
491→        whenever(pageStore.getPagesWithLocalChanges(siteModel)).thenReturn(listOf())
492→    }

494→    private fun createUploadStarter(
495→        connectionStatus: LiveData<ConnectionStatus> = createConnectionStatusLiveData(null),
496→        uploadServiceFacade: UploadServiceFacade = this.uploadServiceFacade,
497→        postUtilsWrapper: PostUtilsWrapper = createMockedPostUtilsWrapper(),
498→        uploadStore: UploadStore = createMockedUploadStore(0),
499→        dispatcher: Dispatcher = mock()
500→    ) = UploadStarter(
501→        context = mock(),
502→        postStore = postStore,
503→        pageStore = pageStore,
504→        siteStore = siteStore,
505→        bgDispatcher = testDispatcher(),
506→        ioDispatcher = testDispatcher(),
507→        networkUtilsWrapper = createMockedNetworkUtilsWrapper(),
508→        connectionStatus = connectionStatus,
509→        uploadServiceFacade = uploadServiceFacade,
510→        uploadActionUseCase = UploadActionUseCase(uploadStore, postUtilsWrapper, uploadServiceFacade),
511→        tracker = mock(),
512→        dispatcher = dispatcher
513→    )
514→
515→    private companion object Fixtures {
516→        fun createMockedNetworkUtilsWrapper() = mock<NetworkUtilsWrapper> {
517→            on { isNetworkAvailable() } doReturn true
518→        }
519→
520→        fun createConnectionStatusLiveData(initialValue: ConnectionStatus?): MutableLiveData<ConnectionStatus> {
521→            return MutableLiveData<ConnectionStatus>().apply {
522→                value = initialValue
523→            }
524→        }
525→
526→        fun createMockedPostUtilsWrapper() = mock<PostUtilsWrapper> {
527→            on { isPublishable(any()) } doReturn true
528→            on { isPostInConflictWithRemote(any()) } doReturn false
529→        }
530→
531→        fun createMockedUploadStore(numberOfAutoUploadAttempts: Int) = mock<UploadStore> {
532→            on { getNumberOfPostAutoUploadAttempts(any()) } doReturn numberOfAutoUploadAttempts
533→        }
534→
535→        fun createMockedUploadServiceFacade() = mock<UploadServiceFacade> {
536→            on { isPostUploadingOrQueued(any()) } doReturn false
537→        }
538→
539→        fun createMockedProcessLifecycleOwner(lifecycle: Lifecycle = mock()) = mock<ProcessLifecycleOwner> {
540→            on { this.lifecycle } doReturn lifecycle
541→        }
542→
543→        fun createLocallyChangedPostModel(postStatus: PostStatus = DRAFT, page: Boolean = false) = PostModel().apply {
544→            setId(Random.nextInt())
545→            setTitle(UUID.randomUUID().toString())
546→            setStatus(postStatus.toString())
547→            setIsLocallyChanged(true)
548→            setDateLocallyChanged(DateTimeUtils.iso8601FromTimestamp(Date().time / 1000))
549→            setIsPage(page)
550→        }
551→
552→        fun createSiteModel(isWpCom: Boolean = true) = SiteModel().apply {
553→            setIsWPCom(isWpCom)
554→        }
555→    }
556→}

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks
  2→
  3→import android.content.Intent
  4→import androidx.appcompat.app.AppCompatActivity
  5→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
  6→import org.wordpress.android.ui.ActivityLauncher
  7→import org.wordpress.android.ui.ActivityNavigator
  8→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.LoginForResult
  9→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenEditor
 10→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenEditorForPost
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenEditorForSite
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenInBrowser
 13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenInReader
 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenJetpackForDeepLink
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenLoginPrologue
 16→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenMySite
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenNotifications
 18→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenPages
 19→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenPagesForSite
 20→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenQRCodeAuthFlow
 21→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenReader
 22→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenStats
 23→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenStatsForSite
 24→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenStatsForSiteAndTimeframe
 25→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.OpenStatsForTimeframe
 26→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.ShowSignInFlow
 27→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.StartCreateSiteFlow
 28→import org.wordpress.android.ui.deeplinks.DeepLinkNavigator.NavigateAction.ViewPostInReader
 29→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.misc.SiteCreationSource.DEEP_LINK
 30→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitemonitor.SiteMonitorType
 31→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.StatsTimeframe
 32→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.utils.StatsLaunchedFrom
 33→import org.wordpress.android.util.UriWrapper
 34→import javax.inject.Inject
 35→
 36→class DeepLinkNavigator
 37→@Inject constructor(private val activityNavigator: ActivityNavigator) {
 38→    @Suppress("ComplexMethod", "LongMethod")
 39→    fun handleNavigationAction(navigateAction: NavigateAction, activity: AppCompatActivity) {
 40→        when (navigateAction) {
 41→            LoginForResult -> ActivityLauncher.loginForDeeplink(activity)
 42→            StartCreateSiteFlow -> {
 43→                ActivityLauncher.showMainActivityAndSiteCreationActivity(activity, DEEP_LINK)
 44→            }
 45→            ShowSignInFlow -> ActivityLauncher.showSignInForResultWpComOnly(activity)
 46→            OpenEditor -> ActivityLauncher.openEditorInNewStack(activity)
 47→            is OpenEditorForSite -> ActivityLauncher.openEditorForSiteInNewStack(
 48→                activity,
 49→                navigateAction.site
 50→            )
 51→            is OpenInBrowser -> {
 52→                val browserIntent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, navigateAction.uri.uri)
 53→                activity.startActivity(browserIntent)
 54→            }
 55→            is OpenEditorForPost -> ActivityLauncher.openEditorForPostInNewStack(
 56→                activity,
 57→                navigateAction.site,
 58→                navigateAction.postId
 59→            )
 60→            OpenStats -> ActivityLauncher.viewStatsInNewStack(activity)
 61→            is OpenStatsForTimeframe -> ActivityLauncher.viewStatsForTimeframeInNewStack(
 62→                activity,
 63→                navigateAction.statsTimeframe
 64→            )
 65→
 66→            is OpenStatsForSite -> ActivityLauncher.viewStatsInNewStack(
 67→                activity,
 68→                navigateAction.site,
 69→                StatsLaunchedFrom.LINK
 70→            )
 71→
 72→            is OpenStatsForSiteAndTimeframe -> ActivityLauncher.viewStatsInNewStack(
 73→                activity,
 74→                navigateAction.site,
 75→                navigateAction.statsTimeframe,
 76→                StatsLaunchedFrom.LINK
 77→            )
 78→
 79→            OpenReader -> ActivityLauncher.viewReaderInNewStack(activity)
 80→            is OpenInReader -> ActivityLauncher.viewPostDeeplinkInNewStack(activity, navigateAction.uri.uri)
 81→            is ViewPostInReader -> ActivityLauncher.viewReaderPostDetailInNewStack(
 82→                activity,
 83→                navigateAction.blogId,
 84→                navigateAction.postId,
 85→                navigateAction.uri.uri
 86→            )
 87→            OpenNotifications -> ActivityLauncher.viewNotificationsInNewStack(activity)
 88→            is OpenPagesForSite -> ActivityLauncher.viewPagesInNewStack(activity, navigateAction.site)
 89→            OpenPages -> ActivityLauncher.viewPagesInNewStack(activity)
 90→            is OpenQRCodeAuthFlow -> ActivityLauncher.startQRCodeAuthFlowInNewStack(activity, navigateAction.uri)
 91→            OpenMySite -> ActivityLauncher.viewMySiteInNewStack(activity)
 92→            OpenLoginPrologue -> ActivityLauncher.showLoginPrologue(activity)
 93→            is OpenJetpackForDeepLink ->
 94→                ActivityLauncher.openJetpackForDeeplink(activity, navigateAction.action, navigateAction.uri)
 95→            is NavigateAction.OpenJetpackStaticPosterView ->
 96→                ActivityLauncher.showJetpackStaticPoster(activity)
 97→            is NavigateAction.OpenMediaForSite -> activityNavigator.openMediaInNewStack(activity, navigateAction.site)
 98→            NavigateAction.OpenMedia -> activityNavigator.openMediaInNewStack(activity)
 99→            is NavigateAction.OpenMediaPickerForSite -> activityNavigator.openMediaPickerInNewStack(
100→                activity,
101→                navigateAction.site
102→            )
103→            NavigateAction.DomainManagement -> ActivityLauncher.openDomainManagement(activity)
104→            is NavigateAction.OpenSiteMonitoringForSite -> activityNavigator.openSiteMonitoringInNewStack(
105→                activity,
106→                navigateAction.site,
107→                navigateAction.siteMonitorType
108→            )
109→            is NavigateAction.OpenMySiteWithMessage -> activityNavigator.openMySiteWithMessageInNewStack(
110→                activity,
111→                navigateAction.message
112→            )
113→        }
114→        if (navigateAction != LoginForResult) {
115→            activity.finish()
116→        }
117→    }
118→
119→    sealed class NavigateAction {
120→        object LoginForResult : NavigateAction()
121→        data class OpenInBrowser(val uri: UriWrapper) : NavigateAction()
122→        data class OpenEditorForPost(val site: SiteModel, val postId: Int) : NavigateAction()
123→        data class OpenEditorForSite(val site: SiteModel) : NavigateAction()
124→        object OpenReader : NavigateAction()
125→        data class OpenInReader(val uri: UriWrapper) : NavigateAction()
126→        data class ViewPostInReader(val blogId: Long, val postId: Long, val uri: UriWrapper) : NavigateAction()
127→        object OpenEditor : NavigateAction()
128→        data class OpenStatsForSiteAndTimeframe(val site: SiteModel, val statsTimeframe: StatsTimeframe) :
129→            NavigateAction()
130→
131→        data class OpenStatsForSite(val site: SiteModel) : NavigateAction()
132→        data class OpenStatsForTimeframe(val statsTimeframe: StatsTimeframe) : NavigateAction()
133→        object OpenStats : NavigateAction()
134→        object StartCreateSiteFlow : NavigateAction()
135→        object ShowSignInFlow : NavigateAction()
136→        object OpenNotifications : NavigateAction()
137→        data class OpenPagesForSite(val site: SiteModel) : NavigateAction()
138→        object OpenPages : NavigateAction()
139→        data class OpenQRCodeAuthFlow(val uri: String) : NavigateAction()
140→        object OpenMySite : NavigateAction()
141→        object OpenLoginPrologue : NavigateAction()
142→        data class OpenJetpackForDeepLink(val action: String?, val uri: UriWrapper) : NavigateAction()
143→        object OpenJetpackStaticPosterView : NavigateAction()
144→        data class OpenMediaForSite(val site: SiteModel) : NavigateAction()
145→        object OpenMedia : NavigateAction()
146→        data class OpenMediaPickerForSite(val site: SiteModel) : NavigateAction()
147→        object DomainManagement : NavigateAction()
148→        data class OpenSiteMonitoringForSite(val site: SiteModel?, val siteMonitorType: SiteMonitorType) :
149→            NavigateAction()
150→        data class OpenMySiteWithMessage(val message: Int) : NavigateAction()
151→    }
152→}
153→

 87→) : ScopedViewModel(mainDispatcher) {

 89→    val isRefreshing: LiveData<Boolean> = _isRefreshing
 90→
 91→    private var isInitialized = false
 92→

 96→        singleEvent = true
 97→    )

109→

111→    val statsModuleUiModel: LiveData<Event<StatsModuleUiModel>> = _statsModuleUiModel
112→

114→    val showJetpackPoweredBottomSheet: LiveData<Event<Boolean>> = _showJetpackPoweredBottomSheet

119→    fun start(intent: Intent, restart: Boolean = false) {
120→        val localSiteId = intent.getIntExtra(WordPress.LOCAL_SITE_ID, 0)
121→
122→        val timeframe = intent.getSerializableExtraCompat<StatsTimeframe>(StatsActivity.ARG_DESIRED_TIMEFRAME)
123→        val launchedFrom = intent.getSerializableExtraCompat<StatsLaunchedFrom>(StatsActivity.ARG_LAUNCHED_FROM)
124→        val initialTimeFrame = getInitialTimeFrame(timeframe, launchedFrom)
125→        val initialGranularity = intent.getSerializableExtraCompat<StatsGranularity>(StatsActivity.ARG_GRANULARITY)
126→        val initialSelectedPeriod = intent.getStringExtra(StatsActivity.INITIAL_SELECTED_PERIOD_KEY)
127→        val notificationType = intent.getSerializableExtraCompat<NotificationType>(ARG_NOTIFICATION_TYPE)
128→        start(
129→            localSiteId,
130→            launchedFrom,
131→            initialTimeFrame,
132→            initialSelectedPeriod,
133→            restart,
134→            notificationType,
135→            initialGranularity
136→        )
137→    }
138→
139→    fun onSaveInstanceState(outState: Bundle) {
140→        selectedDateProvider.onSaveInstanceState(outState)
141→    }
142→
143→    fun onRestoreInstanceState(savedState: Bundle?) {
144→        if (savedState != null) {
145→            selectedDateProvider.onRestoreInstanceState(savedState)
146→        } else {
147→            selectedDateProvider.clear()
148→            statsSiteProvider.reset()
149→        }

152→    private fun getInitialTimeFrame(timeframe: StatsTimeframe?, launchedFrom: StatsLaunchedFrom?): StatsSection? {
153→        if (statsTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig.isEnabled() && launchedFrom == StatsLaunchedFrom.LINK) {
154→            setupDeeplinkForTrafficTab(timeframe)
155→        }
156→
157→        return when (timeframe) {
158→            StatsTimeframe.TRAFFIC -> StatsSection.TRAFFIC
159→            StatsTimeframe.INSIGHTS -> StatsSection.INSIGHTS
160→            StatsTimeframe.SUBSCRIBERS -> StatsSection.SUBSCRIBERS
161→            DAY -> StatsSection.DAYS
162→            WEEK -> StatsSection.WEEKS
163→            MONTH -> StatsSection.MONTHS
164→            YEAR -> StatsSection.YEARS
165→            else -> null
166→        }
167→    }
168→
169→    private fun setupDeeplinkForTrafficTab(timeframe: StatsTimeframe?) {
170→        when (timeframe) {
171→            DAY -> selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(StatsGranularity.DAYS)
172→            WEEK -> selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(StatsGranularity.WEEKS)
173→            MONTH -> selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(StatsGranularity.MONTHS)
174→            YEAR -> selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(StatsGranularity.YEARS)
175→            else -> { /* Do nothing */ }
176→        }
177→    }
178→
179→    @Suppress("ComplexMethod", "LongParameterList")
180→    fun start(
181→        localSiteId: Int,
182→        launchedFrom: StatsLaunchedFrom?,
183→        initialSection: StatsSection?,
184→        initialSelectedPeriod: String?,
185→        restart: Boolean,
186→        notificationType: NotificationType?,
187→        granularity: StatsGranularity? = null
188→    ) {
189→        if (restart) {
190→            selectedDateProvider.clear()
191→        }
192→        // Check if VM is not already initialized
193→        if (!isInitialized || restart) {
194→            isInitialized = true
195→
196→            analyticsTracker.trackStatsAccessed(
197→                site = statsSiteProvider.siteModel,
198→                tapSource = launchedFrom?.value ?: ""
199→            )
200→
201→            initialSection?.let {
202→                statsSectionManager.setSelectedSection(it)
203→
204→                val trafficGranularity = it.toStatsGranularity()
205→                if (statsTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig.isEnabled() && trafficGranularity != null) {
206→                    selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(trafficGranularity)
207→                }
208→            }
209→            granularity?.let {
210→                if (it != selectedTrafficGranularityManager.getSelectedTrafficGranularity()) {
211→                    selectedTrafficGranularityManager.setSelectedTrafficGranularity(it)
212→                }
213→            }
214→            updateSelectedSectionByTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig()
215→            trackSectionSelected(statsSectionManager.getSelectedSection())
216→
217→            val initialGranularity = granularity ?: initialSection?.toStatsGranularity()
218→            if (initialGranularity != null && initialSelectedPeriod != null) {
219→                selectedDateProvider.setInitialSelectedPeriod(initialGranularity, initialSelectedPeriod)
220→            }
221→
222→            if (launchedFrom == StatsLaunchedFrom.WIDGET) {
223→                analyticsTracker.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.STATS_WIDGET_TAPPED, statsSiteProvider.siteModel)
224→            }
225→
226→            if (notificationType != null) {
227→                notificationsTracker.trackTappedNotification(notificationType)
228→            }
229→        }
230→
231→        _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsEnabledViewUiModel())
232→
233→        val siteChanged = statsSiteProvider.start(localSiteId)
234→        if (!isStatsModuleEnabled()) {
235→            _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsDisabledViewUiModel())
236→        } else {
237→            if (restart && siteChanged) {
238→                launch {
239→                    listUseCases.forEach { useCase ->
240→                        useCase.value.onCleared()
241→                        useCase.value.refreshData(true)
242→                    }
243→                }
244→            }
245→        }
246→
247→        if (BuildConfig.IS_JETPACK_APP) {
248→            updateRevampedInsights()
249→        }
250→
251→        if (jetpackBrandingUtils.shouldShowJetpackPoweredBottomSheet()) showJetpackPoweredBottomSheet()
252→
253→        if (jetpackFeatureRemovalOverlayUtil.shouldShowFeatureSpecificJetpackOverlay(STATS))
254→            showJetpackOverlay()
255→    }
256→
257→    private fun updateSelectedSectionByTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig() {
258→        if (statsTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig.isEnabled()) {
259→            val selectedSection = statsSectionManager.getSelectedSection()
260→            val isSelectedSectionRemoved = selectedSection == StatsSection.DAYS ||
261→                    selectedSection == StatsSection.WEEKS ||
262→                    selectedSection == StatsSection.MONTHS ||
263→                    selectedSection == StatsSection.YEARS
264→
265→            if (isSelectedSectionRemoved) {
266→                // statsTrafficSubscribersTabFeatureConfig has just been enabled. Update the cached selected section.
267→                statsSectionManager.setSelectedSection(StatsSection.TRAFFIC)
268→            }
269→        }
270→    }
271→
272→    private fun showJetpackOverlay() {
273→        _showJetpackOverlay.value = Event(true)
274→    }
275→
276→    private fun showJetpackPoweredBottomSheet() {
277→//        _showJetpackPoweredBottomSheet.value = Event(true)
278→    }
279→
280→    private fun updateRevampedInsights() {
281→        val insightsUseCase = listUseCases[StatsSection.INSIGHTS]
282→        insightsUseCase?.launch(defaultDispatcher) {
283→            val insightTypes = statsStore.getAddedInsights(statsSiteProvider.siteModel)
284→            if (insightTypes.containsAll(DEFAULT_INSIGHTS)) { // means not upgraded to new insights
285→                // The new set of default cards is added at the top of their list and preserve their additions
286→                val addedInsightTypes = insightTypes - DEFAULT_INSIGHTS.toSet()
287→                val updateInsightTypes: MutableSet<InsightType> = mutableSetOf()
288→                updateInsightTypes.addAll(JETPACK_DEFAULT_INSIGHTS)
289→                updateInsightTypes.addAll(addedInsightTypes)
290→                statsStore.updateTypes(statsSiteProvider.siteModel, updateInsightTypes.toList())
291→                insightsUseCase.loadData()
292→            }
293→        }
294→    }
295→
296→    private fun isStatsModuleEnabled() =
297→        statsSiteProvider.siteModel.isActiveModuleEnabled("stats") || statsSiteProvider.siteModel.isWPCom
298→
299→    private fun loadData(executeLoading: suspend () -> Unit) = launch {
300→        _isRefreshing.value = true
301→
302→        executeLoading()
303→
304→        _isRefreshing.value = false
305→    }
306→
307→    @SuppressLint("NullSafeMutableLiveData")
308→    fun onPullToRefresh() {
309→        _showSnackbarMessage.value = null
310→        statsSiteProvider.clear()
311→        if (networkUtilsWrapper.isNetworkAvailable()) {
312→            loadData {
313→                val baseListUseCase = listUseCases[statsSectionManager.getSelectedSection()]
314→                baseListUseCase?.refreshTypes()
315→                baseListUseCase?.refreshData(true)
316→            }
317→        } else {
318→            _isRefreshing.value = false
319→            _showSnackbarMessage.value = SnackbarMessageHolder(UiStringRes(R.string.no_network_title))
320→        }
321→    }
322→
323→    fun onSiteChanged() {
324→        if (!isStatsModuleEnabled()) {
325→            _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsDisabledViewUiModel())
326→        } else {
327→            _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsEnabledViewUiModel())
328→            loadData {
329→                listUseCases.values.forEach {
330→                    it.refreshData(true)
331→                }
332→            }
333→        }
334→    }
335→
336→    fun onSectionSelected(statsSection: StatsSection) {
337→        statsSectionManager.setSelectedSection(statsSection)
338→
339→        listUseCases[statsSection]?.onListSelected()
340→
341→        trackSectionSelected(statsSection)
342→    }

344→    private fun trackSectionSelected(statsSection: StatsSection) {
345→        when (statsSection) {
346→            StatsSection.TRAFFIC -> analyticsTracker.trackWithGranularity(
347→                STATS_PERIOD_ACCESSED,
348→                selectedTrafficGranularityManager.getSelectedTrafficGranularity()
349→            )
350→
351→            StatsSection.INSIGHTS -> analyticsTracker.track(STATS_INSIGHTS_ACCESSED)
352→
353→            StatsSection.SUBSCRIBERS -> analyticsTracker.track(STATS_SUBSCRIBERS_ACCESSED)
354→
355→            StatsSection.DAYS -> analyticsTracker.trackWithGranularity(
356→                STATS_PERIOD_DAYS_ACCESSED,
357→                StatsGranularity.DAYS
358→            )
359→
360→            StatsSection.WEEKS -> analyticsTracker.trackWithGranularity(
361→                STATS_PERIOD_WEEKS_ACCESSED,
362→                StatsGranularity.WEEKS
363→            )
364→
365→            StatsSection.MONTHS -> analyticsTracker.trackWithGranularity(
366→                STATS_PERIOD_MONTHS_ACCESSED,
367→                StatsGranularity.MONTHS
368→            )
369→
370→            StatsSection.YEARS -> analyticsTracker.trackWithGranularity(
371→                STATS_PERIOD_YEARS_ACCESSED,
372→                StatsGranularity.YEARS
373→            )
374→
375→            StatsSection.ANNUAL_STATS -> Unit // Do nothing
376→            StatsSection.DETAIL -> Unit // Do nothing
377→            StatsSection.INSIGHT_DETAIL -> Unit // Do nothing
378→            StatsSection.TOTAL_LIKES_DETAIL -> Unit // Do nothing
379→            StatsSection.TOTAL_COMMENTS_DETAIL -> Unit // Do nothing
380→            StatsSection.TOTAL_FOLLOWERS_DETAIL -> Unit // Do nothing
381→        }
382→    }

390→    fun onEnableStatsModuleClick() {
391→        _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsActivatingViewUiModel())
392→        launch {
393→            when (statsModuleActivateUseCase.postActivateStatsModule(statsSiteProvider.siteModel)) {
394→                is NetworkUnavailable -> {
395→                    _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsDisabledViewUiModel())
396→                    _showSnackbarMessage.value = SnackbarMessageHolder(UiStringRes(R.string.no_network_title))
397→                }
398→                is RemoteRequestFailure -> {
399→                    _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsDisabledViewUiModel())
400→                    _showSnackbarMessage.value =
401→                        SnackbarMessageHolder(UiStringRes(R.string.stats_disabled_enable_stats_error_message))
402→                }
403→                is Success -> {
404→                    _statsModuleUiModel.value = Event(buildShowStatsEnabledViewUiModel())
405→                }
406→            }
407→        }
408→    }
409→
410→    private fun buildShowStatsEnabledViewUiModel() = StatsModuleUiModel(disabledStatsViewVisible = false)
411→
412→    private fun buildShowStatsDisabledViewUiModel() =
413→        StatsModuleUiModel(disabledStatsViewVisible = true, disabledStatsProgressVisible = false)
414→
415→    private fun buildShowStatsActivatingViewUiModel() =
416→        StatsModuleUiModel(disabledStatsViewVisible = true, disabledStatsProgressVisible = true)
417→
418→    data class DateSelectorUiModel(
419→        val isVisible: Boolean = false,
420→        val isGranularitySpinnerVisible: Boolean = false,
421→        val date: String? = null,
422→        val timeZone: String? = null,
423→        val enableSelectPrevious: Boolean = false,
424→        val enableSelectNext: Boolean = false
425→    )
426→
427→    data class StatsModuleUiModel(
428→        val disabledStatsViewVisible: Boolean = false,
429→        val disabledStatsProgressVisible: Boolean = false
430→    )
431→}

## Summary of Changes

### 1. StatsLinkHandler.kt
Added deep link support for "traffic" and "subscribers" paths in the `toS...]
I have successfully implemented the deep link support for the Subscribers tab and Traffic tab. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/deeplinks/handlers/StatsLinkHandler.kt`
Added support for "traffic" and "subscribers" paths in the `toStatsTimeframe()` function:
- `"traffic"` → `StatsTimeframe.TRAFFIC`
- `"subscribers"` → `StatsTimeframe.SUBSCRIBERS`

This enables deep links like:
- `wordpress://stats/traffic` or `https://wordpress.com/stats/traffic/$site` → Opens Traffic tab
- `wordpress://stats/subscribers` or `https://wordpress.com/stats/subscribers/$site` → Opens Subscribers tab

### 2. `WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/deeplinks/handlers/StatsLinkHandlerTest.kt`

Added a release note entry for version 24.9 documenting the new feature.

## How It Works

The existing infrastructure in `StatsViewModel` already supports `TRAFFIC` and `SUBSCRIBERS` timeframes (in the `getInitialTimeFrame()` method, lines 152-167). The `StatsTimeframe` enum also already had these values defined. The only missing piece was the mapping in `StatsLinkHandler` from URL path strings to the corresponding `StatsTimeframe` enum values.

The deep link behavior now supports:
1. **Traffic tab deep links**: `wordpress://stats/traffic` or `https://wordpress.com/stats/traffic/$site`
2. **Subscribers tab deep links**: `wordpress://stats/subscribers` or `https://wordpress.com/stats/subscribers/$site`
3. **Traffic tab with granularity**: When the Traffic tab feature is enabled, deep links with granularity (day/week/month/year) will automatically set the appropriate granularity within the Traffic tab (handled by `StatsViewModel.setupDeeplinkForTrafficTab()`)

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.61 (commit: 44a38b08).

## Issue to Resolve

🤖 Cancel stale requests on domain search screen
When searching for a new domain (to purchase) within the domain management flow, the results of multiple requests are loaded sequentially after typing. Only the latest request should be used to update the pending / loading screen.

Note that this is a separate concern from the debounce delay (used to throttle requests and reduce wasted fetches on keystrokes). Increasing the debounce delay _could_ mitigate the buggy results ux, but this has a tradeoff of increasing the total time to success. Instead, we should ensure that only the response to the latest request can update the ui state.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/domains/management/newdomainsearch/NewDomainSearchViewModel.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Now I understand the issue. The problem is in the `fetchDomains` function being called via `onEach`. When multiple search queries are debounced and sent, each one will make an API call, and the responses may come back out of order. If an earlier request takes longer than a later request, the stale results will overwrite the newer results.

The solution is to use `mapLatest` instead of `onEach`. The `mapLatest` operator will automatically cancel any previous execution when a new value arrives, ensuring only the latest request's results are used.

## Summary of Changes

**Problem:** When searching for a new domain, if multiple requests were made in quick succession (e.g., due to rapid typing), the responses could come back out of order. This caused stale results from earlier requests to potentially overwrite newer results, leading to a buggy user experience.

**Solution:** Changed from `onEach` to `mapLatest` operator in the Kotlin Flow pipeline.

### Key Changes to `NewDomainSearchViewModel.kt`:

1. **Added `ExperimentalCoroutinesApi` import** - Required for `mapLatest` operator
2. **Replaced `onEach` import with `mapLatest` import** 
3. **Updated `@OptIn` annotation** - Added `ExperimentalCoroutinesApi::class` alongside `FlowPreview::class`
4. **Changed `.onEach(::fetchDomains)` to `.mapLatest { fetchDomains(it) }`** 

### How it works:

The `mapLatest` operator automatically cancels any previous in-flight execution when a new value arrives in the flow. This means:

- When a user types a new character that triggers a new search request
- If there's already a search request in progress
- The previous request's coroutine will be cancelled
- Only the latest request will update the UI state

This is separate from the debounce mechanism (which reduces the number of API calls by waiting for typing pauses), and instead ensures that even if multiple requests do get sent, only the most recent response will be used.

The existing tests should continue to pass since they use mocked responses that return immediately, and the core debouncing behavior remains unchanged.

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.reader.utils
  2→
  3→import androidx.collection.SparseArrayCompat
  4→import androidx.collection.forEach
  5→import androidx.collection.isNotEmpty
  6→import androidx.collection.set
  7→import org.wordpress.android.R
  8→import org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTag
  9→import org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTagList
 10→import org.wordpress.android.models.ReaderTagType
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.compose.components.menu.dropdown.MenuElementData
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString
 13→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.indexOrNull
 14→import javax.inject.Inject
 15→
 16→class ReaderTopBarMenuHelper @Inject constructor() {
 17→    fun createMenu(readerTagsList: ReaderTagList): List<MenuElementData> {
 18→        return mutableListOf<MenuElementData>().apply {
 19→            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isDiscover }?.let { discoverIndex ->
 20→                add(createDiscoverItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(discoverIndex)))
 21→            }
 22→            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isFollowedSites }?.let { followingIndex ->
 23→                add(createSubscriptionsItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(followingIndex)))
 24→            }
 25→            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isBookmarked }?.let { savedIndex ->
 26→                add(createSavedItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(savedIndex)))
 27→            }
 28→            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isPostsILike }?.let { likedIndex ->
 29→                add(createLikedItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(likedIndex)))
 30→            }
 31→            readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isA8C }?.let { a8cIndex ->
 32→                add(createAutomatticItem(getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(a8cIndex)))
 33→            }
 34→            readerTagsList
 35→                .foldIndexed(SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>()) { index, sparseArray, readerTag ->
 36→                    if (readerTag.tagType == ReaderTagType.CUSTOM_LIST) {
 37→                        sparseArray[index] = readerTag
 38→                    }
 39→                    sparseArray
 40→                }
 41→                .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
 42→                ?.let { customListsArray ->
 43→                    add(MenuElementData.Divider)
 44→                    add(createCustomListsItem(customListsArray))
 45→                }
 46→        }
 47→    }
 48→
 49→    private fun createDiscoverItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 50→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 51→            id = id,
 52→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_discover),
 53→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_discover_24dp,
 54→        )
 55→    }
 56→
 57→    private fun createSubscriptionsItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 58→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 59→            id = id,
 60→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_subscriptions),
 61→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_subscriptions_24dp,
 62→        )
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun createSavedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 66→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 67→            id = id,
 68→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_saved),
 69→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_saved_24dp,
 70→        )
 71→    }
 72→
 73→    private fun createLikedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 74→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 75→            id = id,
 76→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_liked),
 77→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_liked_24dp,
 78→        )
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    private fun createAutomatticItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 82→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 83→            id = id,
 84→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_automattic),
 85→        )
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    private fun createCustomListsItem(customLists: SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>): MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu {
 89→        val customListsMenuItems = mutableListOf<MenuElementData.Item.Single>()
 90→        customLists.forEach { index, readerTag ->
 91→            customListsMenuItems.add(
 92→                MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 93→                    id = getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(index),
 94→                    text = UiString.UiStringText(readerTag.tagTitle),
 95→                )
 96→            )
 97→        }
 98→        return MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu(
 99→            // We don't need this ID since this menu item just opens the sub-menu. It doesn't
100→            // change the content that is currently being displayed.
101→            id = "custom-lists",
102→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_lists),
103→            children = customListsMenuItems,
104→        )
105→    }
106→
107→    private fun getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(readerTagIndex: Int): String = "$readerTagIndex"
108→
109→    fun getReaderTagIndexFromMenuItem(menuItem: MenuElementData.Item.Single) = menuItem.id.toInt()
110→}

 18→@Singleton
 19→@MainThread
 20→@Suppress("ForbiddenComment")
 21→class ReaderTracker @Inject constructor(
 22→    private val dateProvider: DateProvider,
 23→    private val appPrefsWrapper: AppPrefsWrapper,
 24→    private val analyticsTrackerWrapper: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper,
 25→    private val analyticsUtilsWrapper: AnalyticsUtilsWrapper
 26→) {
 27→    // TODO: evaluate to use something like Dispatchers.Main.Immediate in the fun(s)
 28→    // to sync the access to trackers; so to remove the @MainThread and make the
 29→    // usage of this class more transparent to its users
 30→    private val trackers = mutableMapOf<ReaderTrackerType, ReaderTrackerInfo>()
 31→
 32→    init {
 33→        for (trackerType in ReaderTrackerType.values()) {
 34→            trackers[trackerType] = ReaderTrackerInfo()
 35→        }
 36→    }
 37→
 38→    fun setupTrackers() {
 39→        for (key in trackers.keys) {
 40→            trackers[key] = ReaderTrackerInfo()
 41→        }
 42→    }
 43→
 44→    fun start(type: ReaderTrackerType) {
 45→        trackers[type]?.let {
 46→            if (!isRunning(type)) {
 47→                AppLog.d(AppLog.T.MAIN, "ReaderTracker: started $type")
 48→                trackers[type] = it.copy(startDate = dateProvider.getCurrentDate())
 49→            }
 50→        }
 51→    }
 52→
 53→    fun stop(type: ReaderTrackerType) {
 54→        trackers[type]?.let { trackerInfo ->
 55→            if (isRunning(type)) {
 56→                AppLog.d(AppLog.T.MAIN, "ReaderTracker: stopped $type")
 57→                trackerInfo.startDate?.let { startDate ->
 58→                    val accumulatedTime = trackerInfo.accumulatedTime +
 59→                            DateTimeUtils.secondsBetween(dateProvider.getCurrentDate(), startDate)
 60→                    // let reset the startDate to null
 61→                    trackers[type] = ReaderTrackerInfo(accumulatedTime = accumulatedTime)
 62→                } ?: AppLog.e(AppLog.T.READER, "ReaderTracker > stop found a null startDate")
 63→            }
 64→        }
 65→    }
 66→
 67→    fun isRunning(type: ReaderTrackerType): Boolean {
 68→        return trackers[type]?.startDate != null
 69→    }
 70→
 71→    fun getAnalyticsData(): Map<String, Any> {
 72→        return trackers.entries.associate { it.key.propertyName to it.value.accumulatedTime }
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    fun trackReaderTabIfNecessary(readerTab: ReaderTab) {
 76→        if (readerTab != appPrefsWrapper.getReaderActiveTab()) {
 77→            when (readerTab) {
 78→                ReaderTab.FOLLOWING -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_FOLLOWING_SHOWN)
 79→                ReaderTab.DISCOVER -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_DISCOVER_SHOWN)
 80→                ReaderTab.LIKED -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_LIKED_SHOWN)
 81→                ReaderTab.SAVED -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(
 82→                    AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_SAVED_LIST_SHOWN,
 83→                    mapOf("source" to "reader_filter")
 84→                )
 85→                ReaderTab.A8C -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_A8C_SHOWN)
 86→                ReaderTab.P2 -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_P2_SHOWN)
 87→                ReaderTab.CUSTOM -> analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(AnalyticsTracker.Stat.READER_CUSTOM_TAB_SHOWN)
 88→            }
 89→            appPrefsWrapper.setReaderActiveTab(readerTab)
 90→        }
 91→    }
 92→
 93→    fun onAppGoesToBackground() {
 94→        appPrefsWrapper.setReaderActiveTab(null)
 95→    }
 96→
 97→    fun onBottomNavigationTabChanged() {
 98→        appPrefsWrapper.setReaderActiveTab(null)
 99→    }
100→
101→    /* TRACK */
102→
103→    fun track(
104→        stat: AnalyticsTracker.Stat
105→    ) {
106→        analyticsTrackerWrapper.track(stat)
107→    }
108→

475→enum class ReaderTab(
476→    val id: Int,
477→    val source: String
478→) {
479→    FOLLOWING(1, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_FOLLOWING),
480→    DISCOVER(2, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_DISCOVER),
481→    LIKED(3, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_LIKED),
482→    SAVED(4, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_SAVED),
483→    CUSTOM(5, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_CUSTOM),
484→    A8C(6, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_A8C),
485→    P2(7, ReaderTracker.SOURCE_P2);
486→
487→    companion object {
488→        fun fromId(id: Int): ReaderTab {
489→            return when (id) {
490→                FOLLOWING.id -> FOLLOWING
491→                DISCOVER.id -> DISCOVER
492→                LIKED.id -> LIKED
493→                SAVED.id -> SAVED
494→                A8C.id -> A8C
495→                P2.id -> P2
496→                CUSTOM.id -> CUSTOM
497→                else -> throw RuntimeException("Unexpected ReaderTab id")
498→            }
499→        }
500→
501→        @JvmStatic
502→        fun transformTagToTab(readerTag: ReaderTag): ReaderTab {
503→            return when {
504→                readerTag.isFollowedSites -> FOLLOWING
505→                readerTag.isPostsILike -> LIKED
506→                readerTag.isBookmarked -> SAVED
507→                readerTag.isDiscover -> DISCOVER
508→                readerTag.isA8C -> A8C
509→                readerTag.isP2 -> P2
510→                else -> CUSTOM
511→            }
512→        }
513→    }
514→}

 38→                .foldIndexed(SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>()) { index, sparseArray, readerTag ->
 39→                    if (readerTag.tagType == ReaderTagType.CUSTOM_LIST) {
 40→                        sparseArray[index] = readerTag
 41→                    }
 42→                    sparseArray
 43→                }
 44→                .takeIf { it.isNotEmpty() }
 45→                ?.let { customListsArray ->
 46→                    add(MenuElementData.Divider)
 47→                    add(createCustomListsItem(customListsArray))
 48→                }
 49→        }
 50→    }
 51→
 52→    private fun createDiscoverItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 53→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 54→            id = id,
 55→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_discover),
 56→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_discover_24dp,
 57→        )
 58→    }
 59→
 60→    private fun createSubscriptionsItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 61→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 62→            id = id,
 63→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_subscriptions),
 64→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_subscriptions_24dp,
 65→        )
 66→    }
 67→
 68→    private fun createSavedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 69→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 70→            id = id,
 71→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_saved),
 72→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_saved_24dp,
 73→        )
 74→    }
 75→
 76→    private fun createLikedItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 77→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 78→            id = id,
 79→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_liked),
 80→            leadingIcon = R.drawable.ic_reader_liked_24dp,
 81→        )
 82→    }
 83→
 84→    private fun createAutomatticItem(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 85→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 86→            id = id,
 87→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_automattic),
 88→        )
 89→    }
 90→
 91→    private fun createP2Item(id: String): MenuElementData.Item.Single {
 92→        return MenuElementData.Item.Single(
 93→            id = id,
 94→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_p2),
 95→        )
 96→    }
 97→
 98→    private fun createCustomListsItem(customLists: SparseArrayCompat<ReaderTag>): MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu {
 99→        val customListsMenuItems = mutableListOf<MenuElementData.Item.Single>()
100→        customLists.forEach { index, readerTag ->
101→            customListsMenuItems.add(
102→                MenuElementData.Item.Single(
103→                    id = getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(index),
104→                    text = UiString.UiStringText(readerTag.tagTitle),
105→                )
106→            )
107→        }
108→        return MenuElementData.Item.SubMenu(
109→            // We don't need this ID since this menu item just opens the sub-menu. It doesn't
110→            // change the content that is currently being displayed.
111→            id = "custom-lists",
112→            text = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.reader_dropdown_menu_lists),
113→            children = customListsMenuItems,
114→        )
115→    }
116→
117→    private fun getMenuItemIdFromReaderTagIndex(readerTagIndex: Int): String = "$readerTagIndex"
118→
119→    fun getReaderTagIndexFromMenuItem(menuItem: MenuElementData.Item.Single) = menuItem.id.toInt()
120→}

[thinking: All the changes have been implemented correctly. Let me summarize what was done:

1. **ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt**: Added handling for P2 tags in the `createMenu()` function by:
   - Adding a check fo...]
All changes have been implemented successfully. Let me provide a summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
The `ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt` was not including the P2 (Followed P2s) feed in the dropdown menu creation logic, even though:
- The `ReaderTag` class already has an `isP2()` method
- The `ReaderTracker` already has `ReaderTab.P2` for tracking
- The tracking method `trackReaderTabIfNecessary` already handles P2 tabs

### Changes Made

1. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/utils/ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`**
   - Added check for P2 tags: `readerTagsList.indexOrNull { it.isP2 }?.let { ... }`
   - Added `createP2Item()` method to create the menu item (without icon, similar to Automattic)

2. **`WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`**
   - Added string resource: `<string name="reader_dropdown_menu_p2">Followed P2s</string>`

3. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/tracker/ReaderTracker.kt`**
   - Added analytics tracking for P2 menu item clicks: `readerTag.isP2 -> "p2"` in `trackDropdownMenuItemTapped()`

4. **`WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/utils/ReaderTopBarMenuHelperTest.kt`**
   - Added `mockP2Tag()` helper function
   - Added test case for when P2 is not present
   - Updated all existing tests to include P2 tag
   - Updated indices accordingly

### Behavior After Fix
- The "Followed P2s" menu item will now appear in the Reader dropdown menu when the user has P2 feeds
- Clicking the item will properly navigate to the P2 feed
- Analytics events will be tracked when the P2 menu item is tapped (with `"id": "p2"`)

[thinking: Both the fix and the new test are in place. Let me provide a summary of the changes made....]
The fix has been implemented. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary

### Root Cause
In `SiteCreationServiceManager.kt`, when a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurred during site creation and it was **NOT** a retry, the code was throwing an `IllegalStateException`. This exception was thrown inside a coroutine but outside the try-catch block that handles exceptions from `createSiteUseCase.createSite()`, causing the app to crash.

This edge case can occur when:
1. The domain suggestion endpoint returns a domain that already exists
2. Two users pick the same domain at the same time and only the first one gets it
3. Network issues where the site was created successfully but the response was lost

### Fix
Changed line 139 in `SiteCreationServiceManager.kt` from:
```kotlin
throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
```
to:
```kotlin
executePhase(FAILURE)
```

This gracefully handles the error by transitioning to the FAILURE state instead of crashing the app. This is consistent with how other error conditions are handled in the same method (e.g., line 142 for generic errors).

### Test Added
Added a new test `verifySiteExistsErrorWithoutRetryResultsInFailure()` in `SiteCreationServiceManagerTest.kt` that verifies:
- When a `SITE_NAME_EXISTS` error occurs and it's not a retry
- The service should transition through IDLE → CREATE_SITE → FAILURE states
- The app should not crash

### Impact
- **Before**: ~2 users per day experienced a crash when this edge case occurred
- **After**: Users will see a failure UI and can retry the site creation, rather than experiencing an app crash

The codebase is at version 23.4 (commit: 1f67fab3).

  9→import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
 10→import androidx.lifecycle.map

 12→import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
 13→import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
 14→import kotlinx.coroutines.Job
 15→import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
 16→import kotlinx.parcelize.Parcelize
 17→import org.wordpress.android.R
 18→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.Dispatcher
 19→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 20→import org.wordpress.android.networking.MShot
 21→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.DomainRegistrationCheckoutWebViewActivity.OpenCheckout.CheckoutDetails
 22→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.DomainRegistrationCompletedEvent
 23→import org.wordpress.android.ui.domains.DomainsRegistrationTracker

 28→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Completed
 29→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.Created
 30→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.CreatedButNotFetched
 31→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.SiteCreationResult.NotCreated
 32→import org.wordpress.android.ui.sitecreation.domains.DomainModel

 39→import org.wordpress.android.util.AppLog.T
