 68→/**
 69→ * Helper class which encapsulates logic for creating UiStates for items in the PostsList.
 70→ */
 71→class PostListItemUiStateHelper @Inject constructor(
 72→    private val appPrefsWrapper: AppPrefsWrapper,
 73→    private val uploadUiStateUseCase: PostModelUploadUiStateUseCase,
 74→    private val labelColorUseCase: PostPageListLabelColorUseCase,
 75→    private val jetpackFeatureRemovalPhaseHelper: JetpackFeatureRemovalPhaseHelper,
 76→    private val blazeFeatureUtils: BlazeFeatureUtils
 77→) {
 78→    @Suppress("LongParameterList", "LongMethod")
 79→    fun createPostListItemUiState(
 80→        authorFilterSelection: AuthorFilterSelection,
 81→        isFilteringByAuthorSupported: Boolean,
 82→        post: PostModel,
 83→        site: SiteModel,
 84→        unhandledConflicts: Boolean,
 85→        hasAutoSave: Boolean,
 86→        capabilitiesToPublish: Boolean,
 87→        statsSupported: Boolean,
 88→        featuredImageUrl: String?,
 89→        formattedDate: String,
 90→        performingCriticalAction: Boolean,
 91→        isSearch: Boolean,
 92→        uploadStatusTracker: PostModelUploadStatusTracker,
 93→        onAction: (PostModel, PostListButtonType, AnalyticsTracker.Stat) -> Unit,
 94→    ): PostListItemUiState {
 95→        val postStatus: PostStatus = PostStatus.fromPost(post)
 96→        val uploadUiState = uploadUiStateUseCase.createUploadUiState(post, site, uploadStatusTracker)
 97→
 98→        val onButtonClicked = { buttonType: PostListButtonType ->
 99→            onAction.invoke(post, buttonType, POST_LIST_BUTTON_PRESSED)
100→        }
101→        val buttonTypes = createButtonTypes(
102→            postStatus = postStatus,
103→            isLocalDraft = post.isLocalDraft,
104→            isLocallyChanged = post.isLocallyChanged,
105→            uploadUiState = uploadUiState,
106→            siteHasCapabilitiesToPublish = capabilitiesToPublish,
107→            statsSupported = statsSupported,
108→            shouldShowStatsInJetpackRemovalPhase =
109→            jetpackFeatureRemovalPhaseHelper.shouldShowPublishedPostStatsButton(),
110→            shouldShowPromoteWithBlaze = blazeFeatureUtils.isPostBlazeEligible(
111→                site,
112→                postStatus,
113→                post
114→            ),
115→        )
116→        // val defaultActions = createDefaultViewActions(buttonTypes, onButtonClicked)
117→        val moreActions = createMoreActions(buttonTypes, onButtonClicked)
118→
119→        val remotePostId = RemotePostId(RemoteId(post.remotePostId))
120→        val localPostId = LocalPostId(LocalId(post.id))
121→        val baseTitle = getTitle(post = post)
122→        val excerpt = getExcerpt(post = post)
123→
124→        val title =
125→            if (baseTitle == UiStringRes(R.string.untitled_in_parentheses) && excerpt != null) null else baseTitle
126→
127→        val postInfo = getPostInfoLabels(
128→            postStatus,
129→            formattedDate,
130→            post.authorDisplayName,
131→            authorFilterSelection,
132→            isSearch,
133→            isFilteringByAuthorSupported
134→        )
135→        val statuses = getStatuses(
136→            postStatus = postStatus,
137→            post = post,
138→            uploadUiState = uploadUiState,
139→            hasUnhandledConflicts = unhandledConflicts,
140→            hasAutoSave = hasAutoSave
141→        )
142→        val statusesColor = labelColorUseCase.getLabelsColor(post, uploadUiState, unhandledConflicts, hasAutoSave)
143→        val statusesDelimiter = UiStringRes(R.string.multiple_status_label_delimiter)
144→        val onSelected = {
145→            when (postStatus) {
146→                TRASHED -> {
147→                    onAction.invoke(
148→                        post,
149→                        BUTTON_SHOW_MOVE_TRASHED_POST_TO_DRAFT_DIALOG,
150→                        POST_LIST_ITEM_SELECTED
151→                    )
152→                }
153→
154→                UNKNOWN, PUBLISHED, DRAFT, PRIVATE, PENDING, SCHEDULED -> onAction.invoke(
155→                    post,
156→                    BUTTON_EDIT,
157→                    POST_LIST_ITEM_SELECTED
158→                )
159→            }
160→        }
161→        val itemUiData = PostListItemUiStateData(
162→            remotePostId = remotePostId,
163→            localPostId = localPostId,
164→            title = title,
165→            excerpt = excerpt,
166→            imageUrl = featuredImageUrl,
167→            postInfo = postInfo,
168→            statuses = statuses,
169→            statusesColor = statusesColor,
170→            statusesDelimiter = statusesDelimiter,
171→            progressBarUiState = getProgressBarState(
172→                uploadUiState = uploadUiState,
173→                performingCriticalAction = performingCriticalAction
174→            ),
175→            showOverlay = shouldShowOverlay(
176→                uploadUiState = uploadUiState,
177→                performingCriticalAction = performingCriticalAction
178→            ),
179→            disableRippleEffect = postStatus == TRASHED
180→        )
181→
182→        return PostListItemUiState(
183→            data = itemUiData,
184→            moreActions = moreActions,
185→            onSelected = onSelected
186→        )
187→    }
188→
189→    @Suppress("LongParameterList")
190→    private fun getPostInfoLabels(
191→        postStatus: PostStatus,
192→        formattedDate: String,
193→        displayName: String?,
194→        authorFilterSelection: AuthorFilterSelection,
195→        isSearch: Boolean,
196→        isAuthorFilterVisible: Boolean
197→    ): List<UiString> {
198→        val uiStrings: MutableList<UiString> = mutableListOf()
199→
200→        if (isSearch) {
201→            val postStatusText = when (postStatus) {
202→                UNKNOWN -> R.string.unknown
203→                PUBLISHED -> R.string.post_status_post_published
204→                DRAFT -> R.string.post_status_draft
205→                PRIVATE -> R.string.post_status_post_private
206→                PENDING -> R.string.post_status_pending_review
207→                TRASHED -> R.string.post_status_post_trashed
208→                SCHEDULED -> R.string.post_status_post_scheduled
209→            }
210→            uiStrings.add(UiStringRes(postStatusText))
211→        }
212→
213→        if (formattedDate.isNotBlank()) {
214→            uiStrings.add(UiStringText(formattedDate))
215→        }
216→        if (isAuthorFilterVisible && authorFilterSelection == EVERYONE && !displayName.isNullOrBlank()) {
217→            uiStrings.add(UiStringText(displayName))
218→        }
219→        return uiStrings
220→    }
221→
222→    private fun getTitle(post: PostModel): UiString {
223→        return if (post.title.isNotBlank()) {
224→            UiStringText(StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(post.title).let { HtmlUtils.stripHtml(it) })
225→        } else UiStringRes(R.string.untitled_in_parentheses)
226→    }
227→
228→    private fun getExcerpt(post: PostModel): UiString? =
229→        PostUtils.getPostListExcerptFromPost(post)
230→            .takeIf { !it.isNullOrBlank() }
231→            ?.let { StringEscapeUtils.unescapeHtml4(it) }
232→            ?.let { PostUtils.collapseShortcodes(it) }
233→            ?.let { UiStringText(getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(it)) }
234→    private fun getTextBeforeLargeSpaceGap(input: String): String {
235→        val parts = input.split(Regex("\\s+\\n+"))
236→        return parts.first()
237→    }
238→
239→    private fun getProgressBarState(
240→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
241→        performingCriticalAction: Boolean
242→    ): ProgressBarUiState {
243→        return if (shouldShowProgress(uploadUiState, performingCriticalAction)) {
244→            if (uploadUiState is UploadingMedia) {
245→                ProgressBarUiState.Determinate(uploadUiState.progress)
246→            } else {
247→                ProgressBarUiState.Indeterminate
248→            }
249→        } else {
250→            ProgressBarUiState.Hidden
251→        }
252→    }
253→
254→    private fun shouldShowProgress(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
255→        return performingCriticalAction || uploadUiState is UploadingPost || uploadUiState is UploadingMedia ||
256→                uploadUiState is UploadQueued
257→    }
258→
259→    private fun getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
260→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
261→        postStatus: PostStatus,
262→        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
263→        hasAutoSave: Boolean
264→    ): MutableList<UiString> {
265→        val labels: MutableList<UiString> = ArrayList()
266→        when {
267→            uploadUiState is UploadFailed -> {
268→                getErrorLabel(uploadUiState, postStatus).let { labels.add(it) }
269→            }
270→
271→            uploadUiState is UploadingPost -> if (uploadUiState.isDraft) {
272→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading_draft))
273→            } else {
274→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_uploading))
275→            }
276→
277→            uploadUiState is UploadingMedia -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.uploading_media))
278→            uploadUiState is UploadQueued -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_queued))
279→            uploadUiState is UploadWaitingForConnection -> {
280→                getWaitingForConnectionStatus(uploadUiState.postStatus)?.let {
281→                    labels.add(it)
282→                }
283→            }
284→
285→            hasUnhandledConflicts -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_is_conflicted))
286→            hasAutoSave -> labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_post_autosave_revision_available))
287→        }
288→        return labels
289→    }
290→
291→    private fun getWaitingForConnectionStatus(postStatus: PostStatus): UiString? {
292→        return when (postStatus) {
293→            UNKNOWN, PUBLISHED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_publish))
294→            PRIVATE -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_private))
295→            PENDING -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_pending))
296→            SCHEDULED -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_scheduled))
297→            DRAFT -> (UiStringRes(R.string.post_waiting_for_connection_draft))
298→            TRASHED -> {
299→                AppLog.e(
300→                    POSTS,
301→                    "Developer error: This state shouldn't happen. Trashed post is in " +
302→                            "UploadWaitingForConnection state."
303→                )
304→                return null
305→            }
306→        }
307→    }
308→
309→    private fun getStatuses(
310→        postStatus: PostStatus,
311→        post: PostModel,
312→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
313→        hasUnhandledConflicts: Boolean,
314→        hasAutoSave: Boolean
315→    ): List<UiString> {
316→        val labels = getErrorAndProgressStatuses(
317→            uploadUiState,
318→            postStatus,
319→            hasUnhandledConflicts,
320→            hasAutoSave
321→        )
322→
323→        // we want to show either single error/progress label or 0-n info labels.
324→        if (labels.isEmpty()) {
325→            if (post.isLocalDraft) {
326→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_draft))
327→            } else if (post.isLocallyChanged) {
328→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.local_changes))
329→            }
330→            if (postStatus == PRIVATE) {
331→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_post_private))
332→            }
333→            if (postStatus == PENDING) {
334→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_pending_review))
335→            }
336→            if (post.sticky) {
337→                labels.add(UiStringRes(R.string.post_status_sticky))
338→            }
339→        }
340→        return labels
341→    }
342→
343→    private fun getErrorLabel(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiString {
344→        return when {
345→            uploadUiState.error.mediaError != null -> getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState, postStatus)
346→            uploadUiState.error.postError != null -> UploadUtils.getErrorMessageResIdFromPostError(
347→                postStatus,
348→                false,
349→                uploadUiState.error.postError,
350→                uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload
351→            )
352→
353→            else -> {
354→                val errorMsg = "MediaError and postError are both null."
355→                if (BuildConfig.DEBUG) {
356→                    throw IllegalStateException(errorMsg)
357→                } else {
358→                    AppLog.e(POSTS, errorMsg)
359→                }
360→                UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic)
361→            }
362→        }
363→    }
364→
365→    private fun getMediaUploadErrorMessage(uploadUiState: UploadFailed, postStatus: PostStatus): UiStringRes {
366→        return when {
367→            uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload -> when (postStatus) {
368→                PUBLISHED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying)
369→                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_retrying_private)
370→                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled_retrying)
371→                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted_retrying)
372→                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_generic_error_retrying)
373→            }
374→
375→            uploadUiState.retryWillPushChanges -> when (postStatus) {
376→                PUBLISHED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published)
377→                PRIVATE -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_published_private)
378→                SCHEDULED -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_scheduled)
379→                PENDING -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post_not_submitted)
380→                DRAFT, TRASHED, UNKNOWN -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
381→            }
382→
383→            else -> UiStringRes(R.string.error_media_recover_post)
384→        }
385→    }
386→
387→    private fun shouldShowOverlay(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState, performingCriticalAction: Boolean): Boolean {
388→        // show overlay when post upload is in progress or (media upload is in progress and the user is not using Aztec)
389→        return performingCriticalAction ||
390→                (uploadUiState is UploadingPost ||
391→                        (!appPrefsWrapper.isAztecEditorEnabled && uploadUiState is UploadingMedia))
392→    }
393→
394→    @Suppress("LongParameterList", "ComplexMethod")
395→    private fun createButtonTypes(
396→        postStatus: PostStatus,
397→        isLocalDraft: Boolean,
398→        isLocallyChanged: Boolean,
399→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
400→        siteHasCapabilitiesToPublish: Boolean,
401→        statsSupported: Boolean,
402→        shouldShowStatsInJetpackRemovalPhase: Boolean,
403→        shouldShowPromoteWithBlaze: Boolean
404→    ): List<PostListButtonType> {
405→        val canRetryUpload = uploadUiState is UploadFailed
406→        val canCancelPendingAutoUpload = (uploadUiState is UploadWaitingForConnection ||
407→                (uploadUiState is UploadFailed && uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload))
408→        val canPublishPost = (canRetryUpload || uploadUiState is NothingToUpload || !canCancelPendingAutoUpload) &&
409→                (isLocallyChanged || isLocalDraft || postStatus == DRAFT ||
410→                        (siteHasCapabilitiesToPublish && postStatus == PENDING))
411→
412→        val canShowStats = statsSupported &&
413→                postStatus == PUBLISHED &&
414→                !isLocalDraft &&
415→                !isLocallyChanged &&
416→                shouldShowStatsInJetpackRemovalPhase
417→        val canShowCopy = postStatus == PUBLISHED || postStatus == DRAFT
418→        val canShowCopyUrlButton = !isLocalDraft && postStatus != TRASHED
419→        val canShowViewButton = !canRetryUpload && postStatus != TRASHED
420→        val canShowPublishButton = canRetryUpload || canPublishPost
421→        val buttonTypes = ArrayList<PostListButtonType>()
422→
423→        if (postStatus != TRASHED) {
424→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_EDIT)
425→        }
426→
427→        if (canCancelPendingAutoUpload) {
428→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD)
429→        }
430→
431→        if (canShowPublishButton) {
432→            buttonTypes.add(
433→                when {
434→                    canRetryUpload -> BUTTON_RETRY
435→                    !siteHasCapabilitiesToPublish -> BUTTON_SUBMIT
436→                    postStatus == SCHEDULED && isLocallyChanged -> BUTTON_SYNC
437→                    else -> BUTTON_PUBLISH
438→                }
439→            )
440→        }
441→
442→        if (canShowViewButton) {
443→            buttonTypes.addViewOrPreviewAction(isLocalDraft || isLocallyChanged)
444→        }
445→
446→        if (canShowStats) {
447→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_STATS)
448→        }
449→
450→        if (canShowCopy) {
451→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_COPY)
452→        }
453→
454→        buttonTypes.addMoveToDraftActionIfAvailable(postStatus)
455→
456→        if (canShowCopyUrlButton) {
457→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_COPY_URL)
458→        }
459→
460→        if (shouldShowPromoteWithBlaze) {
461→            buttonTypes.add(BUTTON_PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE)
462→        }
463→
464→        buttonTypes.addDeletingOrTrashAction(isLocalDraft, postStatus)
465→
466→        return buttonTypes
467→    }
468→
469→    private fun MutableList<PostListButtonType>.addViewOrPreviewAction(shouldShowPreview: Boolean) {
470→        add(if (shouldShowPreview) BUTTON_PREVIEW else BUTTON_VIEW)
471→    }
472→
473→    private fun MutableList<PostListButtonType>.addDeletingOrTrashAction(
474→        isLocalDraft: Boolean,
475→        postStatus: PostStatus
476→    ) {
477→        when {
478→            isLocalDraft -> add(BUTTON_DELETE)
479→            postStatus == TRASHED -> add(BUTTON_DELETE_PERMANENTLY)
480→            postStatus != TRASHED -> add(BUTTON_TRASH)
481→        }
482→    }
483→
484→    private fun MutableList<PostListButtonType>.addMoveToDraftActionIfAvailable(postStatus: PostStatus) {
485→        val canMovePostToDraft = postStatus == PUBLISHED || postStatus == TRASHED
486→
487→        if (canMovePostToDraft) {
488→            add(BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT)
489→        }
490→    }
491→
492→    private fun createMoreActions(
493→        buttonTypes: List<PostListButtonType>,
494→        onButtonClicked: (PostListButtonType) -> Unit
495→    ): PostListItemAction.MoreItem {
496→        val allItems = buttonTypes.map { buttonType: PostListButtonType ->
497→            PostListItemAction.SingleItem(buttonType, onButtonClicked)
498→        }
499→        return PostListItemAction.MoreItem(allItems, onButtonClicked)
500→    }
501→}
502→

 22→import androidx.core.content.ContextCompat
 23→import androidx.core.graphics.drawable.DrawableCompat
 24→import androidx.recyclerview.widget.RecyclerView
 25→import org.wordpress.android.R
 26→import org.wordpress.android.WordPress
 27→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiDimen
 28→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiHelpers
 29→import org.wordpress.android.ui.utils.UiString
 30→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.expandTouchTargetArea
 31→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.getColorFromAttribute
 32→import org.wordpress.android.util.extensions.getDrawableFromAttribute
 33→import org.wordpress.android.util.image.ImageManager
 34→import org.wordpress.android.util.image.ImageType
 35→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.posts.PostListItemAction
 36→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.posts.PostListItemType.PostListItemUiState
 37→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.posts.PostListItemUiStateData
 38→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.uistate.ProgressBarUiState
 39→import java.util.concurrent.atomic.AtomicBoolean
 40→import android.R as AndroidR
 41→
 42→const val POST_LIST_ICON_PADDING = 8
 43→const val MAX_TITLE_EXCERPT_LINES = 2
 44→
 45→sealed class PostListItemViewHolder(
 46→    @LayoutRes layout: Int,
 47→    parent: ViewGroup,
 48→    private val imageManager: ImageManager,
 49→    private val uiHelpers: UiHelpers
 50→) : RecyclerView.ViewHolder(LayoutInflater.from(parent.context).inflate(layout, parent, false)) {
 51→    private val featuredImageView: ImageView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.image_featured)
 52→    private val titleTextView: TextView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.title)
 53→    private val postInfoTextView: TextView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.post_info)
 54→    private val statusesTextView: TextView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.statuses_label)
 55→    private val uploadProgressBar: ProgressBar = itemView.findViewById(R.id.upload_progress)
 56→    private val disabledOverlay: FrameLayout = itemView.findViewById(R.id.disabled_overlay)
 57→    private val container: ConstraintLayout = itemView.findViewById(R.id.container)
 58→    private val excerptTextView: TextView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.excerpt)
 59→    private val moreButton: ImageView = itemView.findViewById(R.id.more)
 60→
 61→    private val selectableBackground: Drawable? = parent.context.getDrawableFromAttribute(
 62→        AndroidR.attr.selectableItemBackground
 63→    )
 64→
 65→    private val noTitle = UiString.UiStringRes(R.string.untitled_in_parentheses)
 66→    /**
 67→     * Url of an image loaded in the `featuredImageView`.
 68→     */
 69→    private var loadedFeaturedImgUrl: String? = null
 70→
 71→    companion object {
 72→        var isClickEnabled = AtomicBoolean(true)
 73→    }
 74→
 75→    abstract fun onBind(item: PostListItemUiState)
 76→
 77→    class Standard(
 78→        parent: ViewGroup,
 79→        imageManager: ImageManager,
 80→        uiHelpers: UiHelpers
 81→    ) : PostListItemViewHolder(R.layout.post_list_item, parent, imageManager, uiHelpers) {
 82→        override fun onBind(item: PostListItemUiState) {
 83→            setBasicValues(item.data)
 84→            setMoreActions(item.moreActions.actions)
 85→
 86→            itemView.setOnClickListener {
 87→                if (isSafeClick(it)) {
 88→                    item.onSelected.invoke()
 89→                }
 90→            }
 91→        }
 92→
 93→        // Purpose of this method is to prevent 2 Editors
 94→        // from being launched simultaneously and then producing a crash
 95→        private fun isSafeClick(view: View): Boolean {
 96→            if (isClickEnabled.getAndSet(false)) {
 97→                view.postDelayed({
 98→                    isClickEnabled.set(true)
 99→                }, 1000)
100→                return true
101→            }
102→            return false
103→        }
104→    }
105→
106→    protected fun setBasicValues(data: PostListItemUiStateData) {
107→        uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(titleTextView, data.title)
108→        uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(excerptTextView, data.excerpt)
109→        updatePostInfoLabel(postInfoTextView, data.postInfo)
110→        uiHelpers.updateVisibility(statusesTextView, data.statuses.isNotEmpty())
111→        updateStatusesLabel(statusesTextView, data.statuses, data.statusesDelimiter, data.statusesColor)
112→        showFeaturedImage(data.imageUrl)
113→        updateProgressBarState(data.progressBarUiState)
114→        uiHelpers.updateVisibility(disabledOverlay, data.showOverlay)
115→        if (data.disableRippleEffect) {
116→            container.background = null
117→        } else {
118→            container.background = selectableBackground
119→        }
120→
121→        if ((data.title != null && data.title != noTitle) && data.excerpt != null) {
122→            titleTextView.maxLines = MAX_TITLE_EXCERPT_LINES
123→            excerptTextView.maxLines = MAX_TITLE_EXCERPT_LINES
124→            titleTextView.viewTreeObserver.addOnPreDrawListener(object : ViewTreeObserver.OnPreDrawListener {
125→                override fun onPreDraw(): Boolean {
126→                    // Remove the listener to avoid multiple callbacks
127→                    titleTextView.viewTreeObserver.removeOnPreDrawListener(this)
128→
129→                    // Get the layout of the title text
130→                    val titleLayout = titleTextView.layout
131→
132→                    // Check if the title occupies more than 2 lines
133→                    when (titleLayout.lineCount) {
134→                        1 -> {
135→                            excerptTextView.maxLines = 2
136→                        }
137→                        else -> {
138→                            excerptTextView.maxLines = 1
139→                            titleTextView.maxLines = 2
140→                        }
141→                    }
142→                    return true
143→                }
144→            })
145→        }
146→    }
147→
148→    protected fun setMoreActions(actions: List<PostListItemAction>) {
149→        moreButton.expandTouchTargetArea(R.dimen.post_list_more_button_extra_padding)
150→        moreButton.setOnClickListener { onMoreClicked(actions, moreButton) }
151→    }
152→
153→    private fun updatePostInfoLabel(view: TextView, uiStrings: List<UiString>?) {
154→        val concatenatedText = uiStrings?.joinToString(separator = "  ·  ") {
155→            uiHelpers.getTextOfUiString(view.context, it)
156→        }
157→        uiHelpers.setTextOrHide(view, concatenatedText)
158→    }
159→
160→    protected fun onMoreClicked(actions: List<PostListItemAction>, v: View) {
161→        val menu = PopupMenu(v.context, v)
162→        actions.forEach { singleItemAction ->
163→            val menuItem = menu.menu.add(
164→                Menu.NONE,
165→                singleItemAction.buttonType.value,
166→                Menu.NONE,
167→                getMenuItemTitleWithIcon(v.context, singleItemAction)
168→            )
169→            menuItem.setOnMenuItemClickListener {
170→                singleItemAction.onButtonClicked.invoke(singleItemAction.buttonType)
171→                true
172→            }
173→        }
174→        menu.show()
175→    }
176→
177→    private fun updateProgressBarState(progressBarUiState: ProgressBarUiState) {
178→        uiHelpers.updateVisibility(uploadProgressBar, progressBarUiState.visibility)
179→        when (progressBarUiState) {
180→            is ProgressBarUiState.Indeterminate -> uploadProgressBar.isIndeterminate = true
181→            is ProgressBarUiState.Determinate -> {
182→                uploadProgressBar.isIndeterminate = false
183→                uploadProgressBar.progress = progressBarUiState.progress
184→            }
185→            is ProgressBarUiState.Hidden -> Unit // Do nothing
186→        }
187→    }
188→
189→    private fun updateStatusesLabel(
190→        view: TextView,
191→        statuses: List<UiString>,
192→        delimiter: UiString,
193→        @ColorRes color: Int?
194→    ) {
195→        val separator = uiHelpers.getTextOfUiString(view.context, delimiter)
196→        view.text = statuses.joinToString(separator) { uiHelpers.getTextOfUiString(view.context, it) }
197→        color?.let { statusColor ->
198→            view.setTextColor(
199→                ContextCompat.getColor(
200→                    itemView.context,
201→                    statusColor
202→                )
203→            )
204→        }
205→    }
206→
207→    private fun showFeaturedImage(imageUrl: String?) {
208→        if (!imageUrl.isNullOrBlank() && imageUrl == loadedFeaturedImgUrl) {
209→            // Suppress blinking as the media upload progresses
210→            return
211→        }
212→        if (imageUrl.isNullOrBlank()) {
213→            featuredImageView.visibility = View.GONE
214→            imageManager.cancelRequestAndClearImageView(featuredImageView)
215→        } else {
216→            featuredImageView.visibility = View.VISIBLE
217→            imageManager.loadImageWithCorners(
218→                featuredImageView,
219→                ImageType.PHOTO_ROUNDED_CORNERS,
220→                imageUrl,
221→                uiHelpers.getPxOfUiDimen(
222→                    WordPress.getContext(),
223→                    UiDimen.UIDimenRes(R.dimen.my_site_post_item_image_corner_radius)
224→                )
225→            )
226→        }
227→        loadedFeaturedImgUrl = imageUrl
228→    }
229→
230→    private fun setTint(context: Context, drawable: Drawable, color: Int): Drawable {
231→        val wrappedDrawable: Drawable = DrawableCompat.wrap(drawable)
232→        if (color != 0) {
233→            val iconColor = context.getColorFromAttribute(color)
234→            DrawableCompat.setTint(wrappedDrawable, iconColor)
235→        }
236→        return wrappedDrawable
237→    }
238→
239→    @Suppress("ComplexMethod")
240→    private fun getMenuItemTitleWithIcon(context: Context, item: PostListItemAction): SpannableStringBuilder {
241→        var icon: Drawable? = setTint(
242→            context,
243→            ContextCompat.getDrawable(context, item.buttonType.iconResId)!!, item.buttonType.colorAttrId
244→        )
245→        // If there's no icon, we insert a transparent one to keep the title aligned with the items which have icons.
246→        if (icon == null) icon = ColorDrawable(Color.TRANSPARENT)
247→        val iconSize: Int = context.resources.getDimensionPixelSize(R.dimen.menu_item_icon_size)
248→        icon.setBounds(0, 0, iconSize, iconSize)
249→        val imageSpan = ImageSpan(icon)
250→
251→        // Add a space placeholder for the icon, before the title.
252→        val menuTitle = context.getText(item.buttonType.textResId)
253→        val ssb = SpannableStringBuilder(
254→            menuTitle.padStart(menuTitle.length + POST_LIST_ICON_PADDING)
255→        )
256→
257→        // Replace the space placeholder with the icon.
258→        ssb.setSpan(imageSpan, 1, 2, 0)
259→        return ssb
260→    }
261→}
262→

1→package org.wordpress.android.ui.posts
 2→
 3→import org.wordpress.android.analytics.AnalyticsTracker.Stat
 4→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.PostModel
 5→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel
 6→import org.wordpress.android.util.analytics.AnalyticsUtils
 7→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType
 8→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD
 9→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_COPY
10→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_COPY_URL
11→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_DELETE
12→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_DELETE_PERMANENTLY
13→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_EDIT
14→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_MORE
15→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT
16→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_PREVIEW
17→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_PUBLISH
18→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_RETRY
19→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_SHOW_MOVE_TRASHED_POST_TO_DRAFT_DIALOG
20→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_STATS
21→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_SUBMIT
22→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_SYNC
23→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_TRASH
24→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_VIEW
25→import org.wordpress.android.widgets.PostListButtonType.BUTTON_PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE
26→
27→fun trackPostListAction(site: SiteModel, buttonType: PostListButtonType, postData: PostModel, statsEvent: Stat) {
28→    val properties = HashMap<String, Any?>()
29→    if (!postData.isLocalDraft) {
30→        properties["post_id"] = postData.remotePostId
31→    }
32→
33→    properties["action"] = when (buttonType) {
34→        BUTTON_EDIT -> {
35→            properties[AnalyticsUtils.HAS_GUTENBERG_BLOCKS_KEY] = PostUtils
36→                .contentContainsGutenbergBlocks(postData.content)
37→            "edit"
38→        }
39→        BUTTON_RETRY -> "retry"
40→        BUTTON_SUBMIT -> "submit"
41→        BUTTON_VIEW -> "view"
42→        BUTTON_PREVIEW -> "preview"
43→        BUTTON_STATS -> "stats"
44→        BUTTON_TRASH -> "trash"
45→        BUTTON_COPY -> "copy"
46→        BUTTON_DELETE,
47→        BUTTON_DELETE_PERMANENTLY -> "delete"
48→        BUTTON_PUBLISH -> "publish"
49→        BUTTON_SYNC -> "sync"
50→        BUTTON_MORE -> "more"
51→        BUTTON_MOVE_TO_DRAFT -> "move_to_draft"
52→        BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD -> "cancel_pending_auto_upload"
53→        BUTTON_SHOW_MOVE_TRASHED_POST_TO_DRAFT_DIALOG -> "show_move_trashed_post_to_draft_post_dialog"
54→        BUTTON_COPY_URL -> "copy_url"
55→        BUTTON_PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE -> "promote_with_blaze"
56→    }
57→
58→    AnalyticsUtils.trackWithSiteDetails(statsEvent, site, properties)
59→}

1046→    private fun createPostModel(

1066→        site: SiteModel = SiteModel(),
1067→        unhandledConflicts: Boolean = false,
1068→        hasAutoSave: Boolean = false,
1069→        capabilitiesToPublish: Boolean = true,
1070→        statsSupported: Boolean = true,
1071→        featuredImageUrl: String? = null,
1072→        formattedDate: String = FORMATTER_DATE,
1073→        performingCriticalAction: Boolean = false,
1074→        isSearch: Boolean = false,
1075→        onAction: (PostModel, PostListButtonType, AnalyticsTracker.Stat) -> Unit = { _, _, _ -> }
1076→    ): PostListItemUiState = helper.createPostListItemUiState(
1077→        authorFilterSelection,
1078→        post = post,
1079→        site = site,
1080→        unhandledConflicts = unhandledConflicts,
1081→        hasAutoSave = hasAutoSave,
1082→        capabilitiesToPublish = capabilitiesToPublish,
1083→        statsSupported = statsSupported,
1084→        featuredImageUrl = featuredImageUrl,
1085→        formattedDate = formattedDate,
1086→        onAction = onAction,
1087→        performingCriticalAction = performingCriticalAction,
1088→        uploadStatusTracker = uploadStatusTracker,
1089→        isSearch = isSearch,
1090→        isFilteringByAuthorSupported = isFilteringByAuthorSupported
1091→    )
1092→
1093→    private fun createFailedUploadUiState(
1094→        uploadError: UploadError = createGenericError(),
1095→        isEligibleForAutoUpload: Boolean = false,
1096→        retryWillPushChanges: Boolean = false
1097→    ): UploadFailed {

  7→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.COPY
  8→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.COPY_LINK
  9→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.DELETE_PERMANENTLY
 10→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.MOVE_TO_DRAFT
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.MOVE_TO_TRASH
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE
 13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.PUBLISH_NOW
 14→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.SET_AS_HOMEPAGE
 15→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.SET_AS_POSTS_PAGE
 16→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.SET_PARENT
 17→import org.wordpress.android.ui.pages.PageItem.Action.VIEW_PAGE
 18→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PageListViewModel.PageListType
 19→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PageListViewModel.PageListType.DRAFTS
 20→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PageListViewModel.PageListType.PUBLISHED
 21→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PageListViewModel.PageListType.SCHEDULED
 22→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PageListViewModel.PageListType.TRASHED
 23→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PostModelUploadUiStateUseCase.PostUploadUiState
 24→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PostModelUploadUiStateUseCase.PostUploadUiState.UploadFailed
 25→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.pages.PostModelUploadUiStateUseCase.PostUploadUiState.UploadWaitingForConnection
 26→import javax.inject.Inject
 27→
 28→class CreatePageListItemActionsUseCase @Inject constructor() {
 29→    @SuppressWarnings("ReturnCount")
 30→    fun setupPageActions(
 31→        listType: PageListType,
 32→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
 33→        siteModel: SiteModel,
 34→        remoteId: Long,
 35→        isPageEligibleForBlaze: Boolean = false
 36→    ): Set<Action> {
 37→        return when (listType) {
 38→            SCHEDULED -> return getScheduledPageActions(uploadUiState)
 39→            PUBLISHED -> return getPublishedPageActions(
 40→                siteModel,
 41→                remoteId,
 42→                listType,
 43→                uploadUiState,
 44→                isPageEligibleForBlaze
 45→            )
 46→            DRAFTS -> getDraftsPageActions(uploadUiState)
 47→            TRASHED -> setOf(MOVE_TO_DRAFT, DELETE_PERMANENTLY)
 48→        }
 49→    }
 50→
 51→    private fun getScheduledPageActions(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState): MutableSet<Action> {
 52→        return mutableSetOf(
 53→            VIEW_PAGE,
 54→            SET_PARENT,
 55→            COPY_LINK,
 56→            MOVE_TO_DRAFT,
 57→            MOVE_TO_TRASH
 58→        ).apply {
 59→            if (canCancelPendingAutoUpload(uploadUiState)) {
 60→                add(CANCEL_AUTO_UPLOAD)
 61→            }
 62→        }
 63→    }
 64→
 65→    private fun canCancelPendingAutoUpload(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState) =
 66→        (uploadUiState is UploadWaitingForConnection ||
 67→                (uploadUiState is UploadFailed && uploadUiState.isEligibleForAutoUpload))
 68→
 69→    private fun getPublishedPageActions(
 70→        siteModel: SiteModel,
 71→        remoteId: Long,
 72→        listType: PageListType,
 73→        uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState,
 74→        isPageEligibleForBlaze: Boolean
 75→    ): MutableSet<Action> {
 76→        return mutableSetOf(
 77→            VIEW_PAGE,
 78→            COPY,
 79→            COPY_LINK,
 80→            SET_PARENT
 81→        ).apply {
 82→            if (siteModel.isUsingWpComRestApi &&
 83→                siteModel.showOnFront == ShowOnFront.PAGE.value &&
 84→                remoteId > 0
 85→            ) {
 86→                if (siteModel.pageOnFront != remoteId) {
 87→                    add(SET_AS_HOMEPAGE)
 88→                }
 89→                if (siteModel.pageForPosts != remoteId) {
 90→                    add(SET_AS_POSTS_PAGE)
 91→                }
 92→            }
 93→
 94→            if (siteModel.pageOnFront != remoteId && listType == PUBLISHED) {
 95→                add(MOVE_TO_DRAFT)
 96→                add(MOVE_TO_TRASH)
 97→            }
 98→
 99→            if (canCancelPendingAutoUpload(uploadUiState)) {
100→                add(CANCEL_AUTO_UPLOAD)
101→            }
102→
103→            if (isPageEligibleForBlaze) {
104→                add(PROMOTE_WITH_BLAZE)
105→            }
106→        }
107→    }
108→
109→    private fun getDraftsPageActions(uploadUiState: PostUploadUiState): MutableSet<Action> {
110→        return mutableSetOf(VIEW_PAGE, SET_PARENT, PUBLISH_NOW, MOVE_TO_TRASH, COPY, COPY_LINK).apply {
111→            if (canCancelPendingAutoUpload(uploadUiState)) {
112→                add(CANCEL_AUTO_UPLOAD)
113→            }
114→        }
115→    }
116→}

Based on my analysis, the implementation for the "UI Modernization Posts: Update context menus" issue appears to be complete. All tasks have been marked as done in the issue, and I've verified the following:

## Summary of Completed Implementation

### 1. ✅ Apply new grouping and ordering for the context menu on each tab
The `PostListItemUiStateHelper.kt` file implements the `createButtonTypes` method which provides proper ordering and grouping based on:
- Post status (Draft, Published, Scheduled, Pending, Trashed, etc.)
- Whether the post is a local draft
- Whether there are local changes
- Upload state (failed, waiting, etc.)
- User capabilities (publish rights)

### 2. ✅ Adjust copy and icons
The `PostListButtonType.kt` enum now uses GB (Gutenberg Block) icons:
- `gb_ic_pencil` - Edit
- `gb_ic_external` - View
- `gb_ic_globe` - Preview/Publish
- `gb_ic_chart_bar` - Stats
- `gb_ic_trash` - Trash/Delete/Delete Permanently
- `gb_ic_update` - Sync
- `gb_ic_more_horizontal` - More
- `gb_ic_post_author` - Submit
- `gb_ic_redo` - Retry
- `gb_ic_move_to` - Move to Draft
- `gb_ic_undo` - Cancel Pending Auto Upload
- `gb_ic_copy` - Duplicate
- `gb_ic_link` - Copy Link
- `ic_promote_with_blaze` - Promote with Blaze (special gradient icon)

### 3. ✅ Apply existing visibility rules
The visibility rules are properly implemented in `createButtonTypes`:
- Stats only shown for published posts when stats are supported
- Copy URL not shown for local drafts or trashed posts
- Publish/Submit based on user capabilities
- Retry shown only for failed uploads
- Cancel pending auto upload shown for pending uploads

### 4. ✅ Move offline actions to the context menu
The `BUTTON_CANCEL_PENDING_AUTO_UPLOAD` action is properly added to the context menu when posts are waiting for connection or have failed but are eligible for auto-upload.

### 5. ✅ Add GB icons to the project
All GB icons are present in `/project/WordPress/src/main/res/drawable/`:
- `gb_ic_link.xml`
- `gb_ic_undo.xml`
- `gb_ic_share.xml`
- `gb_ic_trash.xml`
- `gb_ic_update.xml`
- `gb_ic_move_to.xml`
- `gb_ic_post_author.xml`
- `gb_ic_more_horizontal.xml`
- `gb_ic_more_vertical.xml`
- `gb_ic_redo.xml`
- `gb_ic_pencil.xml`
- `gb_ic_chart_bar.xml`
- `gb_ic_copy.xml`
- `gb_ic_comment.xml`
- `gb_ic_globe.xml`
- `gb_ic_external.xml`

The unit tests in `PostListItemUiStateHelperTest.kt` validate all the expected button orderings for various post states, and no linting errors were found in the implementation files.

**The implementation is complete and correct.** All files are properly configured, icons exist, and the tests validate the expected behavior.

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 25.01 (commit: a24e5d1d).

## Issue to Resolve

[Reader IA] Show lists directly on dropdown menu if there are 2 or fewer
We are currently grouping the Lists in a "Lists" submenu independent of the number of lists the user has, but we should only show that submenu when the user has over 2 lists.

So if the user has 2 or fewer, they should be displayed directly in the menu root.

![image](https://github.com/wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android/assets/5091503/57e663d9-218f-4a58-a447-05a86406ea86)

Internal ref: pcdRpT-4tr-p2#comment-7237

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/reader/utils/ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue

[thinking: The implementation and tests look good. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

1. **In ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt:**
   - Added a constant `MAX_LISTS_WITHOUT_SUBMENU = 2` in a companion object
   - ...]
I have implemented the fix for the issue. Here's a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### 1. `ReaderTopBarMenuHelper.kt`

**Added:**
- A constant `MAX_LISTS_WITHOUT_SUBMENU = 2` in a companion object to define the threshold
- A new method `createCustomListItem()` to create a single custom list menu item
- Renamed `createCustomListsItem()` to `createCustomListsSubmenu()` for clarity

**Modified the logic in `createMenu()`:**
```kotlin
if (customListsArray.size() <= MAX_LISTS_WITHOUT_SUBMENU) {
    // Display lists directly in the menu root
    customListsArray.forEach { index, readerTag ->
        add(createCustomListItem(index, readerTag))
    }
} else {
    // Group lists in a submenu
    add(createCustomListsSubmenu(customListsArray))
}
```

### 2. `ReaderTopBarMenuHelperTest.kt`

**Added three new tests:**
- `GIVEN only one custom list WHEN createMenu THEN list shown directly in menu without submenu` - Verifies 1 list is shown directly
- `GIVEN two custom lists WHEN createMenu THEN lists shown directly in menu without submenu` - Verifies 2 lists are shown directly  
- `GIVEN three custom lists WHEN createMenu THEN lists shown in submenu` - Verifies 3+ lists use a submenu

## Behavior After the Fix

| Number of Lists | Behavior |
|----------------|----------|
| 0 | No lists section shown |
| 1 | List shown directly in menu root (after divider) |
| 2 | Lists shown directly in menu root (after divider) |
| 3+ | Lists grouped in "Lists" submenu (after divider) |

This matches the requirement from the issue: lists should only be shown in a submenu when the user has more than 2 lists.

 40→class PrepublishingHomeViewModel @Inject constructor(
 41→    private val getPostTagsUseCase: GetPostTagsUseCase,
 42→    private val postSettingsUtils: PostSettingsUtils,
 43→    private val getButtonUiStateUseCase: GetButtonUiStateUseCase,
 44→    private val analyticsTrackerWrapper: AnalyticsTrackerWrapper,
 45→    private val storyRepositoryWrapper: StoryRepositoryWrapper,
 46→    private val updateStoryPostTitleUseCase: UpdateStoryPostTitleUseCase,
 47→    private val getCategoriesUseCase: GetCategoriesUseCase,
 48→    @Named(BG_THREAD) private val bgDispatcher: CoroutineDispatcher
 49→) : ScopedViewModel(bgDispatcher) {
 50→    private var isStarted = false
 51→    private var updateStoryTitleJob: Job? = null
 52→    private lateinit var editPostRepository: EditPostRepository
 53→
 54→    private val _storyTitleUiState = MutableLiveData<StoryTitleUiState>()
 55→    val storyTitleUiState: LiveData<StoryTitleUiState> = _storyTitleUiState
 56→
 57→    private val _onActionClicked = MutableLiveData<Event<ActionType>>()
 58→    val onActionClicked: LiveData<Event<ActionType>> = _onActionClicked
 59→
 60→    private val _onSubmitButtonClicked = MutableLiveData<Event<PublishPost>>()
 61→    val onSubmitButtonClicked: LiveData<Event<PublishPost>> = _onSubmitButtonClicked
 62→
 63→    private val _uiState = MutableLiveData<List<PrepublishingHomeItemUiState>>()
 64→    private var _socialUiState: MutableLiveData<SocialUiState> = MutableLiveData(SocialUiState.Hidden)
 65→
 66→    val uiState: LiveData<List<PrepublishingHomeItemUiState>> = merge(
 67→        _uiState,
 68→        _socialUiState
 69→    ) { list, socialUiState ->
 70→        list?.map { item ->
 71→            if (item is SocialUiState) {
 72→                socialUiState ?: SocialUiState.Hidden
 73→            } else {
 74→                item
 75→            }
 76→        }
 77→    }

 79→    fun start(editPostRepository: EditPostRepository, site: SiteModel, isStoryPost: Boolean) {
 80→        this.editPostRepository = editPostRepository
 81→        if (isStarted) return
 82→        isStarted = true
 83→
 84→        setupHomeUiState(editPostRepository, site, isStoryPost)
 85→    }

 87→    private fun setupHomeUiState(
 88→        editPostRepository: EditPostRepository,
 89→        site: SiteModel,
 90→        isStoryPost: Boolean
 91→    ) {
 92→        val prepublishingHomeUiStateList = mutableListOf<PrepublishingHomeItemUiState>().apply {
 93→            if (isStoryPost) {
 94→                _storyTitleUiState.postValue(StoryTitleUiState(
 95→                    storyTitle = UiStringText(StringUtils.notNullStr(editPostRepository.title)),
 96→                    storyThumbnailUrl = storyRepositoryWrapper.getCurrentStoryThumbnailUrl()
 97→                ) { storyTitle ->

 99→                })
100→            } else {
101→                add(
102→                    HeaderUiState(
103→                        UiStringText(site.name),
104→                        StringUtils.notNullStr(site.iconUrl)
105→                    )
106→                )
107→            }
108→
109→            if (editPostRepository.status != PostStatus.PRIVATE) {
110→                showPublicPost(editPostRepository)
111→            } else {
112→                showPrivatePost(editPostRepository)
113→            }
114→
115→            if (!editPostRepository.isPage) {
116→                showNotSetPost(editPostRepository, site)
117→            } else {
118→                UiStringRes(R.string.prepublishing_nudges_home_categories_not_set)
119→            }
120→
121→            val categoriesString = getCategoriesUseCase.getPostCategoriesString(
122→                editPostRepository,
123→                site
124→            )
125→
126→            add(HomeUiState(
127→                navigationAction = PrepublishingScreenNavigation.Categories,
128→                actionResult = if (categoriesString.isNotEmpty()) {
129→                    UiStringText(categoriesString)
130→                } else {
131→                    run { UiStringRes(R.string.prepublishing_nudges_home_categories_not_set) }
132→                },
133→                actionClickable = true,
134→                onNavigationActionClicked = ::onActionClicked
135→            ))
136→
137→            add(SocialUiState.Hidden)
138→
139→            add(getButtonUiStateUseCase.getUiState(editPostRepository, site) { publishPost ->
140→                launch(bgDispatcher) {
141→                    waitForStoryTitleJobAndSubmit(publishPost)
142→                }
143→            })
144→        }.toList()

146→        _uiState.postValue(prepublishingHomeUiStateList)
147→    }

[thinking: Looking at the code, I can see the issue. In the `setupHomeUiState` function, there's a section that checks if the post is a page:

```kotlin
if (!editPostRepository.isPage) {
    showNotSetPost(editP...]
Now I understand the issue. Looking at the code in `setupHomeUiState`, there's a check for pages at line 115-119, but the categories section (lines 121-135) is always added regardless of whether it's a page or post.

In WordPress, pages don't support categories - only posts do. So the categories UI should only be shown for posts, not pages.

The fix is to wrap the categories section with the same condition that checks `!editPostRepository.isPage`.

Let me make this fix:

[thinking: The changes are complete. Let me summarize what was done to fix the issue....]
The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Root Cause
In `PrepublishingHomeViewModel.kt`, the Categories section was always being added to the prepublishing home UI state regardless of whether the content type was a post or a page. In WordPress, pages don't support categories - only posts do.

### Fix Applied

**File: `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/posts/prepublishing/home/PrepublishingHomeViewModel.kt`**

The categories section (lines 121-135) was moved inside the `if (!editPostRepository.isPage)` block, so that both Tags and Categories are only shown for posts, not pages:

```kotlin
// Before (buggy):
if (!editPostRepository.isPage) {
    showNotSetPost(editPostRepository, site)  // Tags
} else {
    UiStringRes(R.string.prepublishing_nudges_home_categories_not_set)  // Dead code
}

// Categories always added regardless of page/post
val categoriesString = getCategoriesUseCase.getPostCategoriesString(...)
add(HomeUiState(navigationAction = PrepublishingScreenNavigation.Categories, ...))
```

```kotlin
// After (fixed):
if (!editPostRepository.isPage) {
    showNotSetPost(editPostRepository, site)  // Tags

    // Categories only for posts
    val categoriesString = getCategoriesUseCase.getPostCategoriesString(...)
    add(HomeUiState(navigationAction = PrepublishingScreenNavigation.Categories, ...))
}
```

**File: `WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/posts/PrepublishingHomeViewModelTest.kt`**

1. Updated the existing test `verify that page home actions are propagated to prepublishingHomeUiState once the viewModel is started` to expect 1 action (Publish only) instead of 2 for pages.

2. Added two new tests:
   - `verify that categories action is propagated to prepublishingHomeUiState once post is not a page` - ensures categories are shown for regular posts
   - `verify that categories action is not propagated to prepublishingHomeUiState once post is a page` - ensures categories are NOT shown for pages

### Expected Behavior After Fix
- **Posts**: Show Publish, Tags, and Categories in the prepublishing modal (3 actions)
- **Pages**: Show only Publish in the prepublishing modal (1 action) - no Tags or Categories

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository. The codebase is at version 24.5 (commit: c188122e). ## Issue to Resolve "(∞)" shouldn't be used for infinite percentages in stats screens ### Expected behavior When the percentage is `∞` for data in stats screens, we should only show the increase. ### Actual behavior When the percentage is `∞` for data in stats screens, it shows `(∞)`. ### Steps to reproduce the behavior 1. Launch the Jetpack app. (WordPress app has the same issue too, but Jetpack has more places showing `(∞)`.) 2. Open Stats from "My Site → HOME → Stats" it "My Site → MENU → Stats". 3. If the Views & Visitors card is not added to the screen, add it by using ⚙️ button at the top end of the screen. 4. Find a case which "Previous Week" had 0 views, and "This week" has more than 0 views. 5. Notice `(∞)` is being shown as the percentage. The problem is also on Total Likes card, Total Comments card, and the bar chart on DAYS, WEEKS, MONTHS, YEARS tabs. ##### Tested on Pixel 4 emulator, Android 11.0, WPAndroid 20.9-rc-2 ## Relevant Files The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue: - `RELEASE-NOTES.txt` - `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/stats/refresh/utils/StatsUtils.kt` ## Instructions Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above. Focus on: - Understanding the root cause of the issue - Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem - Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 35→        )
 36→    )

 38→    fun toFormattedString(number: Long?, startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND): String? {
 39→        return number?.let { toFormattedString(it, startValue) }
 40→    }

 42→    fun toFormattedString(number: Long?, startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND, defaultValue: String): String {
 43→        return number?.let { toFormattedString(it, startValue) } ?: defaultValue
 44→    }

 48→    }

 51→        return number?.let { toFormattedString(it, startValue) } ?: defaultValue
 52→    }
 53→
 54→    fun toFormattedString(number: Double, startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND): String {
 55→        return number.let {
 56→            if (it < startValue && it > (startValue * -1)) {
 57→                val formatter = DecimalFormat.getInstance(localeManager.getLocale())
 58→                formatter.maximumFractionDigits = 1
 59→                formatter.minimumFractionDigits = 0
 60→                formatter.format(it)
 61→            } else {
 62→                toFormattedString(it.toLong(), startValue)
 63→            }
 64→        }
 65→    }

 67→    fun toFormattedString(number: Int?, startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND): String? {

 69→    }

 73→    }

 75→    fun toFormattedString(number: Int, startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND): String {

 77→    }
 78→
 79→    fun toFormattedString(
 80→        number: Long,
 81→        startValue: Int = TEN_THOUSAND
 82→    ): String {
 83→        val isNegative = number < 0
 84→        val safeNumber = abs(
 85→            if (number == java.lang.Long.MIN_VALUE) {
 86→                number + 1
 87→            } else {
 88→                number
 89→            }
 90→        )
 91→        if (safeNumber < startValue) {
 92→            return printNumber(safeNumber, isNegative)
 93→        }
 94→
 95→        val e = suffixes.floorEntry(safeNumber)
 96→        val divideBy = e?.key
 97→        val suffix = e?.value
 98→
 99→        val truncated = safeNumber / (divideBy!! / 10)
100→        val hasDecimal = truncated < 100 && truncated / 10.0 != (truncated / 10).toDouble()
101→        return printNumber(truncated, isNegative, suffix, hasDecimal)
102→    }
103→
104→    private fun printNumber(
105→        number: Long,
106→        isNegative: Boolean,
107→        suffix: Int? = null,
108→        hasDecimal: Boolean = false
109→    ): String {
110→        val formattedNumber =
111→            DecimalFormat.getInstance(localeManager.getLocale()).format(
112→                when {
113→                    suffix != null && hasDecimal -> number / 10.0
114→                    suffix != null -> number / 10
115→                    else -> number
116→                }
117→            )
118→        return if (suffix != null) {
119→            resourceProvider.getString(
120→                suffix,
121→                handleNegativeNumber(
122→                    formattedNumber,
123→                    isNegative
124→                )
125→            )
126→        } else {
127→            handleNegativeNumber(
128→                formattedNumber,
129→                isNegative
130→            )
131→        }
132→    }
133→
134→    private fun handleNegativeNumber(number: String, isNegative: Boolean): String {
135→        return if (isNegative) {
136→            resourceProvider.getString(R.string.negative_prefix, number)
137→        } else {
138→            number
139→        }
140→    }

219→    fun buildChange(
220→        previousValue: Long?,
221→        value: Long,
222→        positive: Boolean,
223→        isFormattedNumber: Boolean
224→    ): String? {
225→        return previousValue?.let {
226→            val difference = value - previousValue
227→            val percentage = when (previousValue) {
228→                value -> "0"
229→                0L -> "∞"
230→                else -> {
231→                    val percentageValue = difference.toFloat() / previousValue
232→                    percentFormatter.format(value = percentageValue, rounding = HALF_UP)
233→                }
234→            }
235→            val formattedDifference = mapLongToString(difference, isFormattedNumber)
236→            if (positive) {
237→                resourceProvider.getString(R.string.stats_traffic_increase, formattedDifference, percentage)
238→            } else {
239→                resourceProvider.getString(R.string.stats_traffic_change, formattedDifference, percentage)
240→            }
241→        }
242→    }
243→
244→    private fun mapLongToString(value: Long, isFormattedNumber: Boolean): String {
245→        return when (isFormattedNumber) {
246→            true -> toFormattedString(value)
247→            false -> value.toString()
248→        }
249→    }
250→}

256→private fun getBarWidth(
257→    views: Double,
258→    maxViews: Double
259→): Int? {
260→    return if (maxViews > 0) {
261→        ((views / maxViews) * 100).toInt()
262→    } else {
263→        null
264→    }
265→}

- `stats_traffic_change`: `%1$s (%2$s)` - for other changes

Now I understand the issue. The format is `+%1$s (%2$s)` for increases and `%1$s (%2$s)` for general changes. When the percentage is "∞", it shows "(∞)" which shouldn't be displayed.

  7→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.lists.sections.BlockListItem.BarChartItem
  8→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.lists.sections.BlockListItem.BarChartItem.Bar
  9→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.lists.sections.BlockListItem.ValueItem
 10→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.lists.sections.BlockListItem.ValueItem.State
 11→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.utils.HUNDRED_THOUSAND
 12→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.utils.StatsDateFormatter
 13→import org.wordpress.android.ui.stats.refresh.utils.StatsUtils
 14→import org.wordpress.android.util.text.PercentFormatter
 15→import org.wordpress.android.viewmodel.ResourceProvider
 16→import java.math.RoundingMode.HALF_UP
 17→import javax.inject.Inject
 18→
 19→class PostDayViewsMapper
 20→@Inject constructor(
 21→    private val resourceProvider: ResourceProvider,
 22→    private val statsUtils: StatsUtils,
 23→    private val statsDateFormatter: StatsDateFormatter,
 24→    private val percentFormatter: PercentFormatter
 25→) {
 26→    fun buildTitle(
 27→        selectedItem: Day,
 28→        previousItem: Day?,
 29→        isLast: Boolean
 30→    ): ValueItem {
 31→        val value = selectedItem.count
 32→        val previousValue = previousItem?.count
 33→        val positive = value >= (previousValue ?: 0)
 34→        val change = buildChange(previousValue, value, positive, isFormattedNumber = true)
 35→        val unformattedChange = buildChange(previousValue, value, positive, isFormattedNumber = false)
 36→
 37→        val state = when {
 38→            isLast -> State.NEUTRAL
 39→            positive -> State.POSITIVE
 40→            else -> State.NEGATIVE
 41→        }
 42→        return ValueItem(
 43→            value = statsUtils.toFormattedString(value, HUNDRED_THOUSAND),
 44→            unit = R.string.stats_views,
 45→            isFirst = true,
 46→            change = change,
 47→            state = state,
 48→            contentDescription = resourceProvider.getString(
 49→                R.string.stats_overview_content_description,
 50→                value,
 51→                resourceProvider.getString(R.string.stats_views),
 52→                statsDateFormatter.printDate(selectedItem.period),
 53→                unformattedChange ?: ""
 54→            )
 55→        )
 56→    }
 57→
 58→    private fun buildChange(
 59→        previousValue: Int?,
 60→        value: Int,
 61→        positive: Boolean,
 62→        isFormattedNumber: Boolean
 63→    ): String? {
 64→        return previousValue?.let {
 65→            val difference = value - previousValue
 66→            val percentage = when (previousValue) {
 67→                value -> "0"
 68→                0 -> "∞"
 69→                else -> {
 70→                    val percentageValue = difference.toFloat() / previousValue
 71→                    percentFormatter.format(value = percentageValue, rounding = HALF_UP)
 72→                }
 73→            }
 74→            val formattedDifference = mapIntToString(difference, isFormattedNumber)
 75→            if (positive) {
 76→                resourceProvider.getString(R.string.stats_traffic_increase, formattedDifference, percentage)
 77→            } else {
 78→                resourceProvider.getString(R.string.stats_traffic_change, formattedDifference, percentage)
 79→            }
 80→        }
 81→    }
 82→
 83→    private fun mapIntToString(value: Int, isFormattedNumber: Boolean): String {
 84→        return when (isFormattedNumber) {
 85→            true -> statsUtils.toFormattedString(value)
 86→            false -> value.toString()
 87→        }
 88→    }
 89→
 90→    fun buildChart(
 91→        dayViews: List<Day>,
 92→        selectedDay: String?,
 93→        onBarSelected: (String?) -> Unit,
 94→        onBarChartDrawn: (visibleBarCount: Int) -> Unit
 95→    ): List<BlockListItem> {
 96→        val chartItems = dayViews.map { day ->
 97→            Bar(
 98→                statsDateFormatter.printGranularDate(day.period, DAYS),
 99→                day.period,
100→                day.count
101→            )
102→        }
103→        val result = mutableListOf<BlockListItem>()
104→
105→        val contentDescriptions = statsUtils.getBarChartEntryContentDescriptions(
106→            R.string.stats_views,
107→            chartItems
108→        )
109→
110→        result.add(
111→            BarChartItem(
112→                chartItems,
113→                selectedItem = selectedDay,
114→                onBarSelected = onBarSelected,
115→                onBarChartDrawn = onBarChartDrawn,
116→                entryContentDescriptions = contentDescriptions
117→            )
118→        )
119→        return result
120→    }
121→}
122→

All changes are complete and there are no linter errors in the code I modified. Here's a summary of the fix:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
When displaying percentage changes in stats screens, if the previous value was 0 and the current value is greater than 0, the app was showing "(∞)" for the infinite percentage (e.g., "+20 (∞)"), which is not user-friendly.

### Solution
When the percentage change is infinite (previous value is 0 and current value > 0), the app now shows just the increase value without the percentage (e.g., "+20" instead of "+20 (∞)").

### Files Modified

1. **`WordPress/src/main/res/values/strings.xml`**
   - Added new string resource `stats_traffic_increase_positive` with value `+%1$s` for displaying positive changes without percentage

2. **`fastlane/resources/values/strings.xml`**
   - Added the same string resource to keep fastlane resources in sync

3. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/stats/refresh/utils/StatsUtils.kt`**
   - Modified `buildChange()` method to detect infinite percentage cases (`previousValue == 0L && value != previousValue`)
   - When infinite, use the new `stats_traffic_increase_positive` string instead of `stats_traffic_increase`

4. **`WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/stats/refresh/lists/detail/PostDayViewsMapper.kt`**
   - Applied the same fix to its `buildChange()` method (which uses `Int` instead of `Long`)

5. **`WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/stats/refresh/utils/StatsUtilsTest.kt`**
   - Updated the test `build change with infinite positive difference` to expect the new behavior

6. **`WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/stats/refresh/lists/detail/PostDayViewsMapperTest.kt`**
   - Updated the test `builds title with infinite positive difference` to expect the new behavior

7. **`RELEASE-NOTES.txt`**
   - Added release note entry for this fix

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.92 (commit: 70eb2d9f).

## Issue to Resolve

[Jetpack Social] Fix share limits being shown for Atomic sites
Share limits shouldn't be shown for Atomic sites in the context of Jetpack Social. There are 3 place to double-check using an Atomic site:

1 - Share limits dashboard card;
2 - Edit post - publish bottom sheet;
3 - Edit post - settings;

See p9F6qB-dLk-p2#comment-56603

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

@RenanLukas Is there a timeline for when this might be fixed that we can share with the user? The ticket is still open and it would be nice if we could give them an update.
@bobmatyas we're going to have a look this week.

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/usecase/social/GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

1→package org.wordpress.android.usecase.social

 3→import org.wordpress.android.fluxc.model.SiteModel

 8→
 9→class GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase @Inject constructor(
10→    private val siteStore: SiteStore,
11→) {
12→    suspend fun execute(siteModel: SiteModel): ShareLimit {
13→        val isShareLimitEnabled =
14→            !siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom
15→                    && !siteModel.isWPComAtomic
16→                    && (siteModel.planActiveFeatures?.split(",")?.doesNotContain(FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000) != false)
17→        val result = siteStore.fetchJetpackSocial(siteModel)
18→        return if (isShareLimitEnabled && result is FetchedJetpackSocialResult.Success) {
19→            with(result.jetpackSocial) {
20→                ShareLimit.Enabled(
21→                    shareLimit = shareLimit,
22→                    publicizedCount = publicizedCount,
23→                    sharedPostsCount = sharedPostsCount,
24→                    sharesRemaining = sharesRemaining,
25→                )
26→            }
27→        } else {
28→            ShareLimit.Disabled
29→        }
30→    }
31→}
32→
33→sealed interface ShareLimit {
34→    data class Enabled(
35→        val shareLimit: Int,
36→        val publicizedCount: Int,
37→        val sharedPostsCount: Int,
38→        val sharesRemaining: Int,
39→    ) : ShareLimit
40→
41→    object Disabled : ShareLimit
42→}
43→
44→private const val FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000 = "social-shares-1000"

12→class PostSocialSharingModelMapper @Inject constructor(
13→    private val stringProvider: StringProvider,
14→) {
15→    fun map(
16→        connections: List<PostSocialConnection>,
17→        shareLimit: ShareLimit,
18→    ): PostSocialSharingModel {
19→        val title = mapTitle(connections)
20→        val description = if (shareLimit is ShareLimit.Enabled) mapDescription(shareLimit) else ""
21→        val iconModels = mapIconModels(connections)
22→        val isLowOnShares = mapIsLowOnShares(shareLimit, connections)
23→        return PostSocialSharingModel(
24→            title = title,
25→            description = description,
26→            iconModels = iconModels,
27→            isLowOnShares = isLowOnShares,
28→        )
29→    }
30→
31→    private fun mapDescription(shareLimit: ShareLimit.Enabled): String {
32→        val sharesRemaining = shareLimit.sharesRemaining.coerceAtLeast(0)
33→        return if (sharesRemaining == 1) {
34→            stringProvider.getString(R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_remaining_one)
35→        } else {
36→            stringProvider.getString(R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_remaining, sharesRemaining)
37→        }
38→    }
39→
40→    private fun mapTitle(connections: List<PostSocialConnection>): String {
41→        val sharingEnabledConnections = connections.filter { it.isSharingEnabled }
42→        return when {
43→            // Sharing to 0 accounts
44→            sharingEnabledConnections.isEmpty() ->
45→                stringProvider.getString(R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_title_not_sharing)
46→            // Sharing to 1 out of 1 account
47→            sharingEnabledConnections.hasOneElement() && connections.hasOneElement() ->
48→                stringProvider.getString(
49→                    R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_title_single_account,
50→                    sharingEnabledConnections.single().externalName
51→                )
52→            // Sharing to all accounts
53→            sharingEnabledConnections.size == connections.size ->
54→                stringProvider.getString(
55→                    R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_title_all_accounts,
56→                    connections.size
57→                )
58→            // Sharing to some accounts
59→            else -> stringProvider.getString(
60→                R.string.jetpack_social_social_shares_title_part_of_the_accounts,
61→                sharingEnabledConnections.size,
62→                connections.size
63→            )
64→        }
65→    }
66→
67→    private fun mapIconModels(connections: List<PostSocialConnection>) =
68→        connections.map { connection ->
69→            val iconResId = PublicizeServiceIcon.fromServiceId(connection.service)?.iconResId
70→            TrainOfIconsModel(
71→                data = iconResId,
72→                alpha = if (connection.isSharingEnabled) 1f else 0.3f
73→            )
74→        }.sortedBy { it.alpha }
75→
76→    private fun mapIsLowOnShares(
77→        shareLimit: ShareLimit,
78→        connections: List<PostSocialConnection>
79→    ): Boolean {
80→        if (shareLimit !is ShareLimit.Enabled) return false
81→
82→        val sharingEnabledConnections = connections.filter { it.isSharingEnabled }
83→        return when {
84→            // No more shares left.
85→            shareLimit.sharesRemaining <= 0 -> true
86→            // Sharing to some accounts, but not enough shares for all.
87→            connections.size > sharingEnabledConnections.size && shareLimit.sharesRemaining < connections.size -> true
88→            // Remaining shares < no. of accounts.
89→            shareLimit.sharesRemaining < connections.size -> true
90→            else -> false
91→        }
92→    }
93→}

The fix is complete. Let me provide a summary of the changes:

## Summary of Changes

### Problem
Share limits for Jetpack Social were being shown for Atomic sites, but they shouldn't be. This affected three areas:
1. Share limits dashboard card
2. Edit post - publish bottom sheet  
3. Edit post - settings

### Root Cause
The `GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase` was only checking `!siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom` to disable share limits, but it wasn't excluding Atomic sites (`isWPComAtomic`). Atomic sites are WP.com hosted sites that behave differently and should not display share limits.

### Fix
**File: `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/usecase/social/GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase.kt`**

Added `&& !siteModel.isWPComAtomic` to the `isShareLimitEnabled` condition:

```kotlin
val isShareLimitEnabled =
    !siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom
            && !siteModel.isWPComAtomic  // NEW: Exclude Atomic sites
            && (siteModel.planActiveFeatures?.split(",")?.doesNotContain(FEATURE_SOCIAL_SHARES_1000) != false)
```

This change ensures that:
- WP.com sites (`isHostedAtWPCom = true`) → Share limits disabled ✓
- Atomic sites (`isWPComAtomic = true`) → Share limits disabled ✓ (NEW)
- Self-hosted sites with `social-shares-1000` feature → Share limits disabled ✓
- Other self-hosted sites → Share limits enabled ✓

### Test Added
**File: `WordPress/src/test/java/org/wordpress/android/usecase/social/GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCaseTest.kt`**

Added a new test case to verify that Atomic sites correctly return `ShareLimit.Disabled`:

```kotlin
@Test
fun `Should return Disabled if site is WPCom Atomic`() = test {
    val siteModel: SiteModel = mock()
    whenever(siteModel.isHostedAtWPCom).thenReturn(false)
    whenever(siteModel.isWPComAtomic).thenReturn(true)
    whenever(siteModel.planActiveFeatures).thenReturn("")
    val expected = ShareLimit.Disabled
    val actual = classToTest.execute(siteModel)
    assertEquals(expected, actual)
}
```

Since all three areas mentioned in the issue (dashboard card, publish bottom sheet, and post settings) use the same `GetJetpackSocialShareLimitStatusUseCase`, this fix resolves the issue in all locations with a single, minimal change.

You are working on the wordpress-mobile/WordPress-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 23.84 (commit: afd37fc8).

## Issue to Resolve

[Prompts] Regression in Prompts responses list pagination
### Expected behavior
When tapping the `View all responses` and scrolling the response list I should be able to get an infinite scroll with proper pagination until I see all responses. New posts are added to the end of the list when loaded. (it was working on version 23.6 and below)

### Actual behavior
When tapping the `View all responses` and scrolling the response list I see the new responses loading once or twice but then no more posts are loaded. Posts shift places when a load happens. (this behavior started on version 23.7)

### Steps to reproduce the behavior
On any Jetpack app build of version 23.7 or later do the following:
1. Install and log into the Jetpack app
2. Make sure the `dailyprompt-XXXX` tag for the current day IS NOT FOLLOWED
3. Go to the dashboard
4. Tap `View all responses` on the Prompts card
5. Scroll the prompt tag responses list for a while
6. **Verify** pagination is not working properly (posts are shifting around)
7. **Verify** pagination stops loading new posts at some point

##### Tested on Emulator, Android 14, JPAndroid 23.7 and later

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `RELEASE-NOTES.txt`
- `WordPress/src/main/java/org/wordpress/android/ui/bloggingprompts/BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider.kt`

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

 8→class BloggingPromptsPostTagProvider @Inject constructor(

12→        tagUrl: String

18→        tagUrl: String

21→        ReaderTagType.FOLLOWED,
22→    )
23→
24→    companion object {
25→        const val BLOGGING_PROMPT_TAG = "dailyprompt"
26→        const val BLOGANUARY_TAG = "bloganuary"
27→    }

 42→
